1→#!/bin/bash
     2→# ============================================================================
     3→# TOPIC EXTRACTION FEATURE
     4→# ============================================================================
     5→# LLM-based conversation analysis for goal tracking and clarity assessment
     6→#
     7→# Functions:
     8→#   topic_extraction_get_clarity()    - Get clarity score from topic.json
     9→#   topic_extraction_analyze()        - Run LLM analysis on transcript
    10→#   topic_extraction_check_cadence()  - Check if cadence-based analysis is due
    11→#   topic_extraction_sleeper_start()  - Launch sleeper polling process
    12→#   topic_extraction_sleeper_loop()   - Sleeper polling loop (background)
    13→#
    14→# Dependencies: lib/common.sh, prompts/*.txt
    15→# ============================================================================
    16→
    17→set -euo pipefail
    18→
    19→# ============================================================================
    20→# PRIVATE HELPERS
    21→# ============================================================================
    22→
    23→# Extract transcript excerpt for topic analysis
    24→# Args: transcript_path
    25→# Output: JSON array of preprocessed messages (configurable line count)
    26→_topic_extraction_extract_excerpt() {
    27→    local transcript_path="$1"
    28→
    29→    if [ ! -f "$transcript_path" ]; then
    30→        log_error "Transcript file not found: $transcript_path"
    31→        echo "[]"
    32→        return 1
    33→    fi
    34→
    35→    # Get configuration
    36→    local line_count
    37→    line_count=$(config_get "TOPIC_EXCERPT_LINES")
    38→    line_count=${line_count:-80}
    39→
    40→    local filter_tools
    41→    filter_tools=$(config_get "TOPIC_FILTER_TOOL_MESSAGES")
    42→    filter_tools=${filter_tools:-true}
    43→
    44→    # Build jq filter for preprocessing
    45→    local jq_filter='.message | select(. != null)'
    46→
    47→    # Optionally filter out tool messages (tool_use and tool_result)
    48→    if [ "$filter_tools" = "true" ]; then
    49→        jq_filter+=' | select((.content | if type == "array" then (.[0].type != "tool_use" and .[0].type != "tool_result") else true end))'
    50→    fi
    51→
    52→    # Strip unnecessary attributes to reduce token usage
    53→    jq_filter+=' | del(.model, .id, .type, .stop_reason, .stop_sequence, .usage)'
    54→
    55→    log_debug "Extracting last $line_count lines with preprocessing (filter_tools=$filter_tools)"
    56→
    57→    # Extract, preprocess, and wrap in array
    58→    tail -n "$line_count" "$transcript_path" | jq -c "$jq_filter" | jq -s '.'
    59→}
    60→
    61→# Load prompt template and substitute transcript and previous topic
    62→# Args: transcript_json, session_id
    63→# Output: Prompt text with substituted transcript and previous topic
    64→_topic_extraction_build_prompt() {
    65→    local transcript_json="$1"
    66→    local session_id="$2"
    67→
    68→    local sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
    69→    local prompt_file="${sidekick_root}/features/prompts/topic-only.txt"
    70→
    71→    if [ ! -f "$prompt_file" ]; then
    72→        log_error "Prompt template not found: $prompt_file"
    73→        return 1
    74→    fi
    75→
    76→    # Read prompt template
    77→    local prompt
    78→    prompt=$(cat "$prompt_file")
    79→
    80→    # Load previous topic.json if exists
    81→    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    82→    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"
    83→    local previous_topic=""
    84→
    85→    if [ -f "$topic_file" ]; then
    86→        previous_topic=$(cat "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    87→        log_debug "Loaded previous topic for comparison"
    88→    else
    89→        log_debug "No previous topic found (first analysis)"
    90→    fi
    91→
    92→    # Substitute placeholders
    93→    # Use bash parameter expansion for safer substitution
    94→    # This handles multi-line JSON and special characters
    95→    prompt="${prompt//\{PREVIOUS_TOPIC\}/$previous_topic}"
    96→    prompt="${prompt//\{TRANSCRIPT\}/$transcript_json}"
    97→
    98→    echo "$prompt"
    99→}
   100→
   101→# Parse LLM output and write topic.json
   102→# Args: session_id, llm_output
   103→_topic_extraction_write_topic() {
   104→    local session_id="$1"
   105→    local llm_output="$2"
   106→
   107→    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
   108→    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"
   109→
   110→    log_debug "Parsing LLM output for topic extraction"
   111→
   112→    # Extract JSON from output (handles markdown wrapping)
   113→    local json_output
   114→    json_output=$(claude_extract_json "$llm_output")
   115→
   116→    if ! json_validate "$json_output"; then
   117→        log_error "Invalid JSON from LLM output"
   118→        return 1
   119→    fi
   120→
   121→    # Select appropriate snarky comment based on clarity
   122→    local clarity
   123→    clarity=$(echo "$json_output" | jq -r '.clarity_score // 5')
   124→
   125→    local snarky_comment
   126→    if [ "$clarity" -ge 7 ] 2>/dev/null; then
   127→        snarky_comment=$(echo "$json_output" | jq -r '.high_clarity_snarky_comment // ""')
   128→    else
   129→        snarky_comment=$(echo "$json_output" | jq -r '.low_clarity_snarky_comment // ""')
   130→    fi
   131→
   132→    # Rebuild JSON with single snarky_comment field
   133→    json_output=$(echo "$json_output" | jq --arg snark "$snarky_comment" '. + {snarky_comment: $snark} | del(.high_clarity_snarky_comment, .low_clarity_snarky_comment)')
   134→
   135→    # Backup existing topic.json before overwriting
   136→    if [ -f "$topic_file" ]; then
   137→        # Get timestamp in milliseconds (fallback to seconds if %N not supported)
   138→        local timestamp_ms
   139→        timestamp_ms=$(date +%s%3N 2>/dev/null || echo "$(date +%s)000")
   140→
   141→        local backup_file="${session_dir}/${timestamp_ms}-topic.json"
   142→        mv "$topic_file" "$backup_file"
   143→        log_debug "Backed up previous topic.json to: ${timestamp_ms}-topic.json"
   144→    fi
   145→
   146→    # Write topic.json
   147→    echo "$json_output" > "$topic_file"
   148→    log_info "Topic file updated: $topic_file"
   149→
   150→    return 0
   151→}
   152→
   153→# ============================================================================
   154→# PUBLIC API
   155→# ============================================================================
   156→
   157→# Get clarity score from topic.json
   158→# Args: session_id
   159→# Returns: Clarity score (1-10) or empty if not found
   160→topic_extraction_get_clarity() {
   161→    local session_id="$1"
   162→
   163→    # Get session directory (handles path resolution)
   164→    local session_dir
   165→    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id") || {
   166→        log_debug "Cannot get session directory for: $session_id"
   167→        echo ""
   168→        return 0
   169→    }
   170→
   171→    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"
   172→
   173→    if [ ! -f "$topic_file" ]; then
   174→        log_debug "No topic file found for session: $session_id"
   175→        echo ""
   176→        return 0
   177→    fi
   178→
   179→    # Validate JSON first
   180→    if ! json_validate "$(cat "$topic_file")"; then
   181→        log_debug "Invalid JSON in topic file for session: $session_id"
   182→        return 1
   183→    fi
   184→
   185→    local clarity
   186→    clarity=$(jq -r '.clarity_score // empty' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
   187→
   188→    echo "$clarity"
   189→}
   190→
   191→# Run LLM-based analysis on transcript
   192→# Args: session_id, transcript_path, project_dir
   193→# Returns: 0 on success, 1 on failure
   194→topic_extraction_analyze() {
   195→    local session_id="$1"
   196→    local transcript_path="$2"
   197→    local project_dir="$3"
   198→
   199→    log_info "Starting topic extraction analysis for session: $session_id"
   200→
   201→    # Check if feature enabled
   202→    if ! config_is_feature_enabled "topic_extraction"; then
   203→        log_debug "Topic extraction feature disabled"
   204→        return 0
   205→    fi
   206→
   207→    # Get configuration
   208→    local model=$(config_get "TOPIC_MODEL")
   209→    model=${model:-haiku-4.5}
   210→
   211→    log_debug "Topic analysis using model: $model"
   212→
   213→    # Extract transcript excerpt
   214→    local transcript_excerpt
   215→    transcript_excerpt=$(_topic_extraction_extract_excerpt "$transcript_path")
   216→
   217→    if [ -z "$transcript_excerpt" ] || [ "$transcript_excerpt" = "[]" ]; then
   218→        log_warn "No transcript excerpt extracted, skipping analysis"
   219→        return 0
   220→    fi
   221→
   222→    # Build prompt
   223→    local prompt
   224→    if ! prompt=$(_topic_extraction_build_prompt "$transcript_excerpt" "$session_id"); then
   225→        log_error "Failed to build prompt"
   226→        return 1
   227→    fi
   228→
   229→    # Invoke Claude
   230→    log_debug "Invoking Claude for topic extraction"
   231→    local llm_output
   232→    if ! llm_output=$(claude_invoke "$model" "$prompt" 30); then
   233→        log_error "Claude invocation failed"
   234→        return 1
   235→    fi
   236→
   237→    # Write topic.json
   238→    if ! _topic_extraction_write_topic "$session_id" "$llm_output"; then
   239→        log_error "Failed to write topic file"
   240→        return 1
   241→    fi
   242→
   243→    # Check if resume generation is needed
   244→    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
   245→    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"
   246→
   247→    local significant_change=$(jq -r '.significant_change // false' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "false")
   248→    local clarity=$(jq -r '.clarity_score // 0' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
   249→
   250→    log_debug "significant_change=$significant_change, clarity=$clarity"
   251→
   252→    if [ "$significant_change" = "true" ] && [ "$clarity" -ge 5 ] 2>/dev/null; then
   253→        log_info "Triggering resume generation (significant change detected, clarity=$clarity)"
   254→        resume_generate_async "$session_id" "$transcript_path" || {
   255→            log_warn "Failed to launch resume generation (non-fatal)"
   256→        }
   257→    else
   258→        log_debug "Resume generation not needed (significant_change=$significant_change, clarity=$clarity)"
   259→    fi
   260→
   261→    log_info "Topic extraction analysis complete"
   262→    return 0
   263→}
   264→
   265→# Check if cadence-based analysis is due
   266→# Args: session_id, transcript_path, project_dir, response_count
   267→# Returns: 0 if analysis should run, 1 if not
   268→# Side effect: Launches background analysis if due
   269→topic_extraction_check_cadence() {
   270→    local session_id="$1"
   271→    local transcript_path="$2"
   272→    local project_dir="$3"
   273→    local response_count="$4"
   274→
   275→    log_debug "Checking cadence for response count: $response_count"
   276→
   277→    # Get current clarity score
   278→    local clarity
   279→    clarity=$(topic_extraction_get_clarity "$session_id")
   280→
   281→    # Default to low clarity if no topic file
   282→    if [ -z "$clarity" ]; then
   283→        clarity=5
   284→    fi
   285→
   286→    # Get cadence thresholds
   287→    local clarity_threshold=$(config_get "TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD")
   288→    clarity_threshold=${clarity_threshold:-7}
   289→
   290→    local high_cadence=$(config_get "TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH")
   291→    high_cadence=${high_cadence:-10}
   292→
   293→    local low_cadence=$(config_get "TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW")
   294→    low_cadence=${low_cadence:-1}
   295→
   296→    # Determine which cadence to use
   297→    local cadence=$low_cadence
   298→    if [ "$clarity" -ge "$clarity_threshold" ] 2>/dev/null; then
   299→        cadence=$high_cadence
   300→    fi
   301→
   302→    log_debug "Clarity: $clarity, threshold: $clarity_threshold, cadence: $cadence"
   303→
   304→    # Check if analysis is due
   305→    if [ $((response_count % cadence)) -eq 0 ]; then
   306→        log_info "Cadence-based analysis due at count $response_count (cadence: $cadence)"
   307→
   308→        # Launch analysis in background directly
   309→        local session_dir
   310→        session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
   311→        local pid_file="${session_dir}/analysis.pid"
   312→        local log_file="${session_dir}/analysis.log"
   313→
   314→        # Check if already running
   315→        if process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
   316→            log_debug "Analysis already running for session ${session_id}"
   317→            return 0
   318→        fi
   319→
   320→        log_debug "Launching analysis process for session ${session_id}"
   321→
   322→        local sidekick_root
   323→        sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
   324→
   325→        nohup bash -c "
   326→            # Source common and feature libraries
   327→            source '$sidekick_root/lib/common.sh'
   328→            source '$sidekick_root/features/topic-extraction.sh'
   329→
   330→            # Re-load config
   331→            config_load
   332→
   333→            # Re-initialize logging
   334→            log_init '$session_id'
   335→
   336→            # Run analysis
   337→            topic_extraction_analyze '$session_id' '$transcript_path' '$project_dir'
   338→        " > /dev/null 2>&1 &
   339→
   340→        local pid=$!
   341→        echo "$pid" > "$pid_file"
   342→        log_debug "Launched analysis with PID ${pid}"
   343→
   344→        return 0
   345→    fi
   346→
   347→    log_debug "Analysis not due (count: $response_count, cadence: $cadence)"
   348→    return 1
   349→}
   350→
   351→# Launch sleeper polling process in background
   352→# Args: session_id, transcript_path, project_dir
   353→# Returns: 0 on success
   354→topic_extraction_sleeper_start() {
   355→    local session_id="$1"
   356→    local transcript_path="$2"
   357→    local project_dir="$3"
   358→
   359→    # Check if sleeper enabled
   360→    local sleeper_enabled=$(config_get "SLEEPER_ENABLED")
   361→    sleeper_enabled=${sleeper_enabled:-true}
   362→
   363→    if [ "$sleeper_enabled" != "true" ]; then
   364→        log_debug "Sleeper disabled, skipping"
   365→        return 0
   366→    fi
   367→
   368→    # Check if sleeper already running
   369→    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
   370→    local pid_file="${session_dir}/sleeper.pid"
   371→
   372→    if [ -f "$pid_file" ]; then
   373→        if process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
   374→            log_debug "Sleeper already running for session: $session_id"
   375→            return 0
   376→        else
   377→            # Stale PID file
   378→            log_debug "Removing stale sleeper PID file"
   379→            rm -f "$pid_file"
   380→        fi
   381→    fi
   382→
   383→    log_info "Launching sleeper process for session: $session_id"
   384→
   385→    # Launch sleeper in background directly
   386→    local log_file="${session_dir}/sleeper.log"
   387→
   388→    # Get real source location for library files (where they actually exist)
   389→    local source_root
   390→    local script_dir
   391→    script_dir=$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
   392→    source_root=$(cd "${script_dir}/.." && pwd)
   393→
   394→    # Get runtime root (respects _SIDEKICK_ROOT override for tests)
   395→    local sidekick_root
   396→    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
   397→
   398→    nohup bash -c "
   399→        # Pass runtime root and project context to background process
   400→        export _SIDEKICK_ROOT='$sidekick_root'
   401→        export CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR='${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}'
   402→
   403→        # Source common and feature libraries from real location
   404→        source '$source_root/lib/common.sh'
   405→        source '$source_root/features/topic-extraction.sh'
   406→
   407→        # Re-load config
   408→        config_load
   409→
   410→        # Re-initialize logging
   411→        log_init '$session_id'
   412→
   413→        # Run sleeper loop
   414→        topic_extraction_sleeper_loop '$session_id' '$transcript_path' '$project_dir'
   415→    " > /dev/null 2>&1 &
   416→
   417→    local pid=$!
   418→    echo "$pid" > "$pid_file"
   419→    log_debug "Launched sleeper with PID ${pid}"
   420→
   421→    return 0
   422→}
   423→
   424→# Sleeper polling loop (runs as background process)
   425→# Args: session_id, transcript_path, project_dir
   426→topic_extraction_sleeper_loop() {
   427→    local session_id="$1"
   428→    local transcript_path="$2"
   429→    local project_dir="$3"
   430→
   431→    log_info "Sleeper loop started for session: $session_id"
   432→
   433→    # Get configuration
   434→    local max_duration=$(config_get "SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION")
   435→    max_duration=${max_duration:-600}
   436→
   437→    local min_size_change=$(config_get "SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE")
   438→    min_size_change=${min_size_change:-500}
   439→
   440→    local min_interval=$(config_get "SLEEPER_MIN_INTERVAL")
   441→    min_interval=${min_interval:-10}
   442→
   443→    local min_sleep=$(config_get "SLEEPER_MIN_SLEEP")
   444→    min_sleep=${min_sleep:-2}
   445→
   446→    local max_sleep=$(config_get "SLEEPER_MAX_SLEEP")
   447→    max_sleep=${max_sleep:-20}
   448→
   449→    # Initialize state
   450→    local last_activity_time=$(date +%s)
   451→    local last_size=0
   452→    local last_analysis_time=0
   453→
   454→    # Main polling loop
   455→    while true; do
   456→        # Check inactivity timeout
   457→        local current_time=$(date +%s)
   458→        local inactive_duration=$((current_time - last_activity_time))
   459→
   460→        if [ $inactive_duration -ge $max_duration ]; then
   461→            log_info "Sleeper inactivity timeout reached ($max_duration seconds), exiting"
   462→            break
   463→        fi
   464→
   465→        # Get current transcript size
   466→        local current_size
   467→        current_size=$(util_get_file_size "$transcript_path" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
   468→
   469→        # Check if transcript changed significantly
   470→        local size_delta=$((current_size - last_size))
   471→        local time_since_analysis=$((current_time - last_analysis_time))
   472→
   473→        if [ $size_delta -ge $min_size_change ] && [ $time_since_analysis -ge $min_interval ]; then
   474→            log_debug "Transcript changed by $size_delta bytes, triggering analysis"
   475→
   476→            # Update activity timestamp
   477→            last_activity_time=$current_time
   478→
   479→            # Run analysis
   480→            if topic_extraction_analyze "$session_id" "$transcript_path" "$project_dir"; then
   481→                last_analysis_time=$current_time
   482→            fi
   483→
   484→            last_size=$current_size
   485→        fi
   486→
   487→        # Determine sleep interval based on clarity score
   488→        # Formula: clarity * 2 seconds (capped between min_sleep and max_sleep)
   489→        local clarity
   490→        clarity=$(topic_extraction_get_clarity "$session_id")
   491→
   492→        local sleep_interval=$min_sleep
   493→        if [ -n "$clarity" ] && [ "$clarity" -gt 0 ] 2>/dev/null; then
   494→            # Calculate: clarity * 2
   495→            sleep_interval=$((clarity * 2))
   496→
   497→            # Cap at max_sleep
   498→            if [ $sleep_interval -gt $max_sleep ]; then
   499→                sleep_interval=$max_sleep
   500→            fi
   501→
   502→            # Ensure minimum
   503→            if [ $sleep_interval -lt $min_sleep ]; then
   504→                sleep_interval=$min_sleep
   505→            fi
   506→
   507→            log_debug "Dynamic sleep interval: ${sleep_interval}s (clarity=${clarity})"
   508→        else
   509→            log_debug "No clarity score, using minimum sleep: ${min_sleep}s"
   510→        fi
   511→
   512→        # Update last size
   513→        last_size=$current_size
   514→
   515→        # Sleep
   516→        sleep $sleep_interval
   517→    done
   518→
   519→    log_info "Sleeper loop exited for session: $session_id"
   520→
   521→    # Clean up PID file
   522→    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
   523→    local pid_file="${session_dir}/sleeper.pid"
   524→    rm -f "$pid_file" 2>/dev/null || true
   525→
   526→    return 0
   527→}
   528→
   529→# Launch resume generation in background
   530→# Args: session_id, transcript_path
   531→# Returns: 0 on success
   532→resume_generate_async() {
   533→    local session_id="$1"
   534→    local transcript_path="$2"
   535→
   536→    log_info "Launching resume generation for session: $session_id"
   537→
   538→    # Get session directory
   539→    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
   540→
   541→    # Get real source location for library files (where they actually exist)
   542→    local source_root
   543→    local script_dir
   544→    script_dir=$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
   545→    source_root=$(cd "${script_dir}/.." && pwd)
   546→
   547→    # Get runtime root (respects _SIDEKICK_ROOT override for tests)
   548→    local sidekick_root
   549→    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
   550→
   551→    # Launch resume generation in background directly
   552→    nohup bash -c "
   553→        # Pass runtime root and project context to background process
   554→        export _SIDEKICK_ROOT='$sidekick_root'
   555→        export CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR='${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}'
   556→
   557→        # Source common library from real location
   558→        source '$source_root/lib/common.sh'
   559→
   560→        # Re-load config
   561→        config_load
   562→
   563→        # Re-initialize logging
   564→        log_init '$session_id'
   565→
   566→        log_info 'Starting resume generation for session: $session_id'
   567→
   568→        # Read current topic.json
   569→        topic_file='$session_dir/topic.json'
   570→        if [ ! -f \"\$topic_file\" ]; then
   571→            log_error 'Topic file not found for resume generation'
   572→            exit 1
   573→        fi
   574→
   575→        current_topic=\$(cat \"\$topic_file\")
   576→

   578→        if [ -f '$transcript_path' ]; then
   579→            transcript_excerpt=\$(tail -n 80 '$transcript_path' | jq -s '.' 2>/dev/null || echo '[]')
   580→        else
   581→            log_warn 'Transcript not found, using empty excerpt'
   582→            transcript_excerpt='[]'
   583→        fi
   584→
   585→        # Load prompt template
   586→        prompt_file='$sidekick_root/features/prompts/generate-resume.txt'
   587→        if [ ! -f \"\$prompt_file\" ]; then
   588→            log_error 'Resume prompt template not found: \$prompt_file'
   589→            exit 1
   590→        fi
   591→
   592→        # Build prompt with both topic and transcript
   593→        prompt=\$(cat \"\$prompt_file\")
   594→        prompt=\"\${prompt//\\{CURRENT_TOPIC\\}/\$current_topic}\"
   595→        prompt=\"\${prompt//\\{TRANSCRIPT\\}/\$transcript_excerpt}\"
   596→
   597→        # Get resume model from config
   598→        resume_model=\$(config_get 'RESUME_MODEL')
   599→        resume_model=\${resume_model:-haiku}
   600→

   602→

   604→        if ! llm_output=\$(claude_invoke \"\$resume_model\" \"\$prompt\" 30); then
   605→            log_error 'Claude invocation failed for resume generation'

   607→        fi

   611→            log_error 'Failed to extract JSON from resume generation output'
   612→            exit 1

   617→            log_error 'Invalid JSON from resume generation'
   618→            exit 1
   619→        fi
   620→
   621→        # Write resume.json
   622→        resume_file='$session_dir/resume.json'
   623→        echo \"\$resume_json\" > \"\$resume_file\"
   624→
   625→        log_info 'Resume file created: \$resume_file'
   626→    " > /dev/null 2>&1 &
   627→

   629→    log_debug "Launched resume generation with PID ${pid}"

   631→    return 0
   632→}
   633→
   634→#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   635→# PLUGIN HOOKS
   636→#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   637→
   638→#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   639→# topic_extraction_on_user_prompt_submit - UserPromptSubmit hook for topic extraction
   640→#
   641→# Ensures the sleeper process is running (watchdog pattern)

   661→        log_warn "Failed to ensure sleeper is running (non-fatal)"

#!/bin/bash
# ============================================================================
# TOPIC EXTRACTION FEATURE
# ============================================================================
# LLM-based conversation analysis for goal tracking and clarity assessment
#
# Functions:
#   topic_extraction_get_clarity()    - Get clarity score from topic.json
#   topic_extraction_analyze()        - Run LLM analysis on transcript
#   topic_extraction_check_cadence()  - Check if cadence-based analysis is due
#   topic_extraction_sleeper_start()  - Launch sleeper polling process
#   topic_extraction_sleeper_loop()   - Sleeper polling loop (background)
#
# Dependencies: lib/common.sh, prompts/*.txt
# ============================================================================

set -euo pipefail

# ============================================================================
# PRIVATE HELPERS
# ============================================================================

# Extract transcript excerpt for topic analysis
# Args: transcript_path
# Output: JSON array of preprocessed messages (configurable line count)
_topic_extraction_extract_excerpt() {
    local transcript_path="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$transcript_path" ]; then
        log_error "Transcript file not found: $transcript_path"
        echo "[]"
        return 1
    fi

    # Get configuration
    local line_count
    line_count=$(config_get "TOPIC_EXCERPT_LINES")
    line_count=${line_count:-80}

    local filter_tools
    filter_tools=$(config_get "TOPIC_FILTER_TOOL_MESSAGES")
    filter_tools=${filter_tools:-true}

    # Build jq filter for preprocessing
    local jq_filter='.message | select(. != null)'

    # Optionally filter out tool messages (tool_use and tool_result)
    if [ "$filter_tools" = "true" ]; then
        jq_filter+=' | select((.content | if type == "array" then (.[0].type != "tool_use" and .[0].type != "tool_result") else true end))'
    fi

    # Strip unnecessary attributes to reduce token usage
    jq_filter+=' | del(.model, .id, .type, .stop_reason, .stop_sequence, .usage)'

    log_debug "Extracting last $line_count lines with preprocessing (filter_tools=$filter_tools)"

    # Extract, preprocess, and wrap in array
    tail -n "$line_count" "$transcript_path" | jq -c "$jq_filter" | jq -s '.'
}

# Load prompt template and substitute transcript and previous topic
# Args: transcript_json, session_id
# Output: Prompt text with substituted transcript and previous topic
_topic_extraction_build_prompt() {
    local transcript_json="$1"
    local session_id="$2"

    local sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
    local prompt_file="${sidekick_root}/features/prompts/topic-only.txt"

    if [ ! -f "$prompt_file" ]; then
        log_error "Prompt template not found: $prompt_file"
        return 1
    fi

    # Read prompt template
    local prompt
    prompt=$(cat "$prompt_file")

    # Load previous topic.json if exists
    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"
    local previous_topic=""

    if [ -f "$topic_file" ]; then
        previous_topic=$(cat "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_debug "Loaded previous topic for comparison"
    else
        log_debug "No previous topic found (first analysis)"
    fi

    # Substitute placeholders
    # Use bash parameter expansion for safer substitution
    # This handles multi-line JSON and special characters
    prompt="${prompt//\{PREVIOUS_TOPIC\}/$previous_topic}"
    prompt="${prompt//\{TRANSCRIPT\}/$transcript_json}"

    echo "$prompt"
}

# Parse LLM output and write topic.json
# Args: session_id, llm_output
_topic_extraction_write_topic() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local llm_output="$2"

    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"

    log_debug "Parsing LLM output for topic extraction"

    # Extract JSON from output (handles markdown wrapping)
    local json_output
    json_output=$(claude_extract_json "$llm_output")

    if ! json_validate "$json_output"; then
        log_error "Invalid JSON from LLM output"
        return 1
    fi

    # Select appropriate snarky comment based on clarity
    local clarity
    clarity=$(echo "$json_output" | jq -r '.clarity_score // 5')

    local snarky_comment
    if [ "$clarity" -ge 7 ] 2>/dev/null; then
        snarky_comment=$(echo "$json_output" | jq -r '.high_clarity_snarky_comment // ""')
    else
        snarky_comment=$(echo "$json_output" | jq -r '.low_clarity_snarky_comment // ""')
    fi

    # Rebuild JSON with single snarky_comment field
    json_output=$(echo "$json_output" | jq --arg snark "$snarky_comment" '. + {snarky_comment: $snark} | del(.high_clarity_snarky_comment, .low_clarity_snarky_comment)')

    # Backup existing topic.json before overwriting
    if [ -f "$topic_file" ]; then
        # Get timestamp in milliseconds (fallback to seconds if %N not supported)
        local timestamp_ms
        timestamp_ms=$(date +%s%3N 2>/dev/null || echo "$(date +%s)000")

        local backup_file="${session_dir}/${timestamp_ms}-topic.json"
        mv "$topic_file" "$backup_file"
        log_debug "Backed up previous topic.json to: ${timestamp_ms}-topic.json"
    fi

    # Write topic.json
    echo "$json_output" > "$topic_file"
    log_info "Topic file updated: $topic_file"

    return 0
}

# ============================================================================
# PUBLIC API
# ============================================================================

# Get clarity score from topic.json
# Args: session_id
# Returns: Clarity score (1-10) or empty if not found
topic_extraction_get_clarity() {
    local session_id="$1"

    # Get session directory (handles path resolution)
    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id") || {
        log_debug "Cannot get session directory for: $session_id"
        echo ""
        return 0
    }

    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"

    if [ ! -f "$topic_file" ]; then
        log_debug "No topic file found for session: $session_id"
        echo ""
        return 0
    fi

    # Validate JSON first
    if ! json_validate "$(cat "$topic_file")"; then
        log_debug "Invalid JSON in topic file for session: $session_id"
        return 1
    fi

    local clarity
    clarity=$(jq -r '.clarity_score // empty' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")

    echo "$clarity"
}

# Run LLM-based analysis on transcript
# Args: session_id, transcript_path, project_dir
# Returns: 0 on success, 1 on failure
topic_extraction_analyze() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"

    log_info "Starting topic extraction analysis for session: $session_id"

    # Check if feature enabled
    if ! config_is_feature_enabled "topic_extraction"; then
        log_debug "Topic extraction feature disabled"
        return 0
    fi

    # Get configuration
    local model=$(config_get "TOPIC_MODEL")
    model=${model:-haiku-4.5}

    log_debug "Topic analysis using model: $model"

    # Extract transcript excerpt
    local transcript_excerpt
    transcript_excerpt=$(_topic_extraction_extract_excerpt "$transcript_path")

    if [ -z "$transcript_excerpt" ] || [ "$transcript_excerpt" = "[]" ]; then
        log_warn "No transcript excerpt extracted, skipping analysis"
        return 0
    fi

    # Build prompt
    local prompt
    if ! prompt=$(_topic_extraction_build_prompt "$transcript_excerpt" "$session_id"); then
        log_error "Failed to build prompt"
        return 1
    fi

    # Invoke Claude
    log_debug "Invoking Claude for topic extraction"
    local llm_output
    if ! llm_output=$(claude_invoke "$model" "$prompt" 30); then
        log_error "Claude invocation failed"
        return 1
    fi

    # Write topic.json
    if ! _topic_extraction_write_topic "$session_id" "$llm_output"; then
        log_error "Failed to write topic file"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check if resume generation is needed
    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"

    local significant_change=$(jq -r '.significant_change // false' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "false")
    local clarity=$(jq -r '.clarity_score // 0' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")

    log_debug "significant_change=$significant_change, clarity=$clarity"

    if [ "$significant_change" = "true" ] && [ "$clarity" -ge 5 ] 2>/dev/null; then
        log_info "Triggering resume generation (significant change detected, clarity=$clarity)"
        resume_generate_async "$session_id" "$transcript_path" || {
            log_warn "Failed to launch resume generation (non-fatal)"
        }
    else
        log_debug "Resume generation not needed (significant_change=$significant_change, clarity=$clarity)"
    fi

    log_info "Topic extraction analysis complete"
    return 0
}

# Check if cadence-based analysis is due
# Args: session_id, transcript_path, project_dir, response_count
# Returns: 0 if analysis should run, 1 if not
# Side effect: Launches background analysis if due
topic_extraction_check_cadence() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"
    local response_count="$4"

    log_debug "Checking cadence for response count: $response_count"

    # Get current clarity score
    local clarity
    clarity=$(topic_extraction_get_clarity "$session_id")

    # Default to low clarity if no topic file
    if [ -z "$clarity" ]; then
        clarity=5
    fi

    # Get cadence thresholds
    local clarity_threshold=$(config_get "TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD")
    clarity_threshold=${clarity_threshold:-7}

    local high_cadence=$(config_get "TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH")
    high_cadence=${high_cadence:-10}

    local low_cadence=$(config_get "TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW")
    low_cadence=${low_cadence:-1}

    # Determine which cadence to use
    local cadence=$low_cadence
    if [ "$clarity" -ge "$clarity_threshold" ] 2>/dev/null; then
        cadence=$high_cadence
    fi

    log_debug "Clarity: $clarity, threshold: $clarity_threshold, cadence: $cadence"

    # Check if analysis is due
    if [ $((response_count % cadence)) -eq 0 ]; then
        log_info "Cadence-based analysis due at count $response_count (cadence: $cadence)"

        # Launch analysis in background directly
        local session_dir
        session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
        local pid_file="${session_dir}/analysis.pid"
        local log_file="${session_dir}/analysis.log"

        # Check if already running
        if process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
            log_debug "Analysis already running for session ${session_id}"
            return 0
        fi

        log_debug "Launching analysis process for session ${session_id}"

        local sidekick_root
        sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

        nohup bash -c "
            # Source common and feature libraries
            source '$sidekick_root/lib/common.sh'
            source '$sidekick_root/features/topic-extraction.sh'

            # Re-load config
            config_load

            # Re-initialize logging
            log_init '$session_id'

            # Run analysis
            topic_extraction_analyze '$session_id' '$transcript_path' '$project_dir'
        " > /dev/null 2>&1 &

        local pid=$!
        echo "$pid" > "$pid_file"
        log_debug "Launched analysis with PID ${pid}"

        return 0
    fi

    log_debug "Analysis not due (count: $response_count, cadence: $cadence)"
    return 1
}

# Launch sleeper polling process in background
# Args: session_id, transcript_path, project_dir
# Returns: 0 on success
topic_extraction_sleeper_start() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"

    # Check if sleeper enabled
    local sleeper_enabled=$(config_get "SLEEPER_ENABLED")
    sleeper_enabled=${sleeper_enabled:-true}

    if [ "$sleeper_enabled" != "true" ]; then
        log_debug "Sleeper disabled, skipping"
        return 0
    fi

    # Check if sleeper already running
    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local pid_file="${session_dir}/sleeper.pid"

    if [ -f "$pid_file" ]; then
        if process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
            log_debug "Sleeper already running for session: $session_id"
            return 0
        else
            # Stale PID file
            log_debug "Removing stale sleeper PID file"
            rm -f "$pid_file"
        fi
    fi

    log_info "Launching sleeper process for session: $session_id"

    # Launch sleeper in background directly
    local log_file="${session_dir}/sleeper.log"

    # Get real source location for library files (where they actually exist)
    local source_root
    local script_dir
    script_dir=$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
    source_root=$(cd "${script_dir}/.." && pwd)

    # Get runtime root (respects _SIDEKICK_ROOT override for tests)
    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    nohup bash -c "
        # Pass runtime root and project context to background process
        export _SIDEKICK_ROOT='$sidekick_root'
        export CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR='${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}'

        # Source common and feature libraries from real location
        source '$source_root/lib/common.sh'
        source '$source_root/features/topic-extraction.sh'

        # Re-load config
        config_load

        # Re-initialize logging
        log_init '$session_id'

        # Run sleeper loop
        topic_extraction_sleeper_loop '$session_id' '$transcript_path' '$project_dir'
    " > /dev/null 2>&1 &

    local pid=$!
    echo "$pid" > "$pid_file"
    log_debug "Launched sleeper with PID ${pid}"

    return 0
}

# Sleeper polling loop (runs as background process)
# Args: session_id, transcript_path, project_dir
topic_extraction_sleeper_loop() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"

    log_info "Sleeper loop started for session: $session_id"

    # Get configuration
    local max_duration=$(config_get "SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION")
    max_duration=${max_duration:-600}

    local min_size_change=$(config_get "SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE")
    min_size_change=${min_size_change:-500}

    local min_interval=$(config_get "SLEEPER_MIN_INTERVAL")
    min_interval=${min_interval:-10}

    local min_sleep=$(config_get "SLEEPER_MIN_SLEEP")
    min_sleep=${min_sleep:-2}

    local max_sleep=$(config_get "SLEEPER_MAX_SLEEP")
    max_sleep=${max_sleep:-20}

    # Initialize state
    local last_activity_time=$(date +%s)
    local last_size=0
    local last_analysis_time=0

    # Main polling loop
    while true; do
        # Check inactivity timeout
        local current_time=$(date +%s)
        local inactive_duration=$((current_time - last_activity_time))

        if [ $inactive_duration -ge $max_duration ]; then
            log_info "Sleeper inactivity timeout reached ($max_duration seconds), exiting"
            break
        fi

        # Get current transcript size
        local current_size
        current_size=$(util_get_file_size "$transcript_path" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")

        # Check if transcript changed significantly
        local size_delta=$((current_size - last_size))
        local time_since_analysis=$((current_time - last_analysis_time))

        if [ $size_delta -ge $min_size_change ] && [ $time_since_analysis -ge $min_interval ]; then
            log_debug "Transcript changed by $size_delta bytes, triggering analysis"

            # Update activity timestamp
            last_activity_time=$current_time

            # Run analysis
            if topic_extraction_analyze "$session_id" "$transcript_path" "$project_dir"; then
                last_analysis_time=$current_time
            fi

            last_size=$current_size
        fi

        # Determine sleep interval based on clarity score
        # Formula: clarity * 2 seconds (capped between min_sleep and max_sleep)
        local clarity
        clarity=$(topic_extraction_get_clarity "$session_id")

        local sleep_interval=$min_sleep
        if [ -n "$clarity" ] && [ "$clarity" -gt 0 ] 2>/dev/null; then
            # Calculate: clarity * 2
            sleep_interval=$((clarity * 2))

            # Cap at max_sleep
            if [ $sleep_interval -gt $max_sleep ]; then
                sleep_interval=$max_sleep
            fi

            # Ensure minimum
            if [ $sleep_interval -lt $min_sleep ]; then
                sleep_interval=$min_sleep
            fi

            log_debug "Dynamic sleep interval: ${sleep_interval}s (clarity=${clarity})"
        else
            log_debug "No clarity score, using minimum sleep: ${min_sleep}s"
        fi

        # Update last size
        last_size=$current_size

        # Sleep
        sleep $sleep_interval
    done

    log_info "Sleeper loop exited for session: $session_id"

    # Clean up PID file
    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local pid_file="${session_dir}/sleeper.pid"
    rm -f "$pid_file" 2>/dev/null || true

    return 0
}

# Launch resume generation in background
# Args: session_id, transcript_path
# Returns: 0 on success
resume_generate_async() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"

    log_info "Launching resume generation for session: $session_id"

    # Get session directory
    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")

    # Get real source location for library files (where they actually exist)
    local source_root
    local script_dir
    script_dir=$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
    source_root=$(cd "${script_dir}/.." && pwd)

    # Get runtime root (respects _SIDEKICK_ROOT override for tests)
    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    # Launch resume generation in background directly
    nohup bash -c "
        # Pass runtime root and project context to background process
        export _SIDEKICK_ROOT='$sidekick_root'
        export CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR='${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}'

        # Source common library from real location
        source '$source_root/lib/common.sh'

        # Re-load config
        config_load

        # Re-initialize logging
        log_init '$session_id'

        log_info 'Starting resume generation for session: $session_id'

        # Read current topic.json
        topic_file='$session_dir/topic.json'
        if [ ! -f \"\$topic_file\" ]; then
            log_error 'Topic file not found for resume generation'
            exit 1
        fi

        current_topic=\$(cat \"\$topic_file\")

        # Extract transcript excerpt (last ~3-5 messages, similar to topic-only)
        if [ -f '$transcript_path' ]; then
            transcript_excerpt=\$(tail -n 80 '$transcript_path' | jq -s '.' 2>/dev/null || echo '[]')
        else
            log_warn 'Transcript not found, using empty excerpt'
            transcript_excerpt='[]'
        fi

        # Load prompt template
        prompt_file='$sidekick_root/features/prompts/generate-resume.txt'
        if [ ! -f \"\$prompt_file\" ]; then
            log_error 'Resume prompt template not found: \$prompt_file'
            exit 1
        fi

        # Build prompt with both topic and transcript
        prompt=\$(cat \"\$prompt_file\")
        prompt=\"\${prompt//\\{CURRENT_TOPIC\\}/\$current_topic}\"
        prompt=\"\${prompt//\\{TRANSCRIPT\\}/\$transcript_excerpt}\"

        # Get resume model from config
        resume_model=\$(config_get 'RESUME_MODEL')
        resume_model=\${resume_model:-haiku}

        log_debug 'Invoking Claude for resume generation (model: \$resume_model)'

        # Invoke Claude
        if ! llm_output=\$(claude_invoke \"\$resume_model\" \"\$prompt\" 30); then
            log_error 'Claude invocation failed for resume generation'
            exit 1
        fi

        # Extract JSON from output
        if ! resume_json=\$(claude_extract_json \"\$llm_output\"); then
            log_error 'Failed to extract JSON from resume generation output'
            exit 1
        fi

        # Validate JSON
        if ! echo \"\$resume_json\" | jq empty 2>/dev/null; then
            log_error 'Invalid JSON from resume generation'
            exit 1
        fi

        # Write resume.json
        resume_file='$session_dir/resume.json'
        echo \"\$resume_json\" > \"\$resume_file\"

        log_info 'Resume file created: \$resume_file'
    " > /dev/null 2>&1 &

    local pid=$!
    log_debug "Launched resume generation with PID ${pid}"

    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PLUGIN HOOKS
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# topic_extraction_on_user_prompt_submit - UserPromptSubmit hook for topic extraction
#
# Ensures the sleeper process is running (watchdog pattern)
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#   $2 - transcript_path
#   $3 - project_dir
#
# Returns:
#   0 on success (non-fatal errors logged)
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
topic_extraction_on_user_prompt_submit() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"

    log_debug "Topic Extraction: UserPromptSubmit hook"

    # Ensure sleeper is running (watchdog pattern)
    # sleeper_start checks if already running and returns early if so
    if ! topic_extraction_sleeper_start "$session_id" "$transcript_path" "$project_dir"; then
        log_warn "Failed to ensure sleeper is running (non-fatal)"
    fi

    return 0
}

log_debug "Topic extraction feature loaded"

1→# ============================================================================
     2→# Sidekick Configuration Defaults
     3→# ============================================================================
     4→#
     5→# This file contains all default configuration values for Sidekick.
     6→# Values can be overridden in:
     7→#   - User config: ~/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf
     8→#   - Project config: $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf
     9→#
    10→# Configuration cascade: this file → user → project (later wins)
    11→#
    12→# Format: Shell KEY=VALUE pairs (sourced by bash)
    13→
    14→# ============================================================================
    15→# FEATURES - Set to false to disable
    16→# ============================================================================
    17→
    18→# Topic extraction: LLM-based conversation analysis
    19→FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=true
    20→
    21→# Resume: Generate snarkified resume topic from previous session
    22→FEATURE_RESUME=true
    23→
    24→# Statusline: Enhanced statusline with topic and metrics
    25→FEATURE_STATUSLINE=true
    26→
    27→# Tracking: Request counting
    28→FEATURE_TRACKING=true
    29→
    30→# Reminder: Periodic static reminder injection (depends on tracking)
    31→FEATURE_REMINDER=true
    32→
    33→# Cleanup: Garbage collection for old session directories
    34→FEATURE_CLEANUP=true
    35→
    36→# ============================================================================
    37→# TOPIC EXTRACTION
    38→# ============================================================================
    39→
    40→# Model to use for topic extraction
    41→# Options: haiku, sonnet, opus
    42→TOPIC_MODEL=haiku
    43→
    44→# Number of transcript lines to analyze (default: 80 ≈ 3-5 messages)
    45→TOPIC_EXCERPT_LINES=80
    46→
    47→# Filter out tool_use and tool_result messages from analysis (reduces noise and tokens)
    48→TOPIC_FILTER_TOOL_MESSAGES=true
    49→
    50→# Cadence for high clarity conversations (responses between analyses)
    51→TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH=10
    52→
    53→# Cadence for low clarity conversations (responses between analyses)
    54→TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW=1
    55→
    56→# Clarity threshold (1-10 scale)
    57→# Conversations with clarity >= threshold use high cadence
    58→# Conversations with clarity < threshold use low cadence
    59→TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD=7
    60→
    61→# ============================================================================
    62→# SLEEPER (Background Polling Process)
    63→# ============================================================================
    64→
    65→# Enable sleeper process
    66→SLEEPER_ENABLED=true
    67→
    68→# Maximum sleeper inactivity timeout (seconds)
    69→# Sleeper will exit after this duration of no transcript activity
    70→SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION=600
    71→
    72→# Minimum transcript size change to trigger analysis (bytes)
    73→SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE=500
    74→
    75→# Minimum interval between analyses (seconds)
    76→SLEEPER_MIN_INTERVAL=10
    77→
    78→# Dynamic sleep interval (based on clarity score)
    79→# Formula: clarity * 2 seconds (capped between min and max)
    80→# - Low clarity (1-2): sleep 2-4 seconds (more frequent checks)
    81→# - Medium clarity (5): sleep 10 seconds
    82→# - High clarity (10): sleep 20 seconds (less frequent checks)
    83→SLEEPER_MIN_SLEEP=2
    84→SLEEPER_MAX_SLEEP=20
    85→
    86→# ============================================================================
    87→# RESUME (Session Continuity)
    88→# ============================================================================
    89→
    90→# Model to use for resume snarkification
    91→# Options: haiku, sonnet, opus
    92→RESUME_MODEL=haiku
    93→
    94→# Minimum clarity score to use previous session's topic
    95→# Only sessions with clarity >= this value are considered for resume
    96→RESUME_MIN_CLARITY=5
    97→
    98→# ============================================================================
    99→# STATUSLINE
   100→# ============================================================================
   101→
   102→# Token budget threshold for percentage calculation
   103→# Statusline will show percentage of this value
   104→STATUSLINE_TOKEN_THRESHOLD=160000
   105→
   106→# ============================================================================
   107→# TRACKING
   108→# ============================================================================
   109→
   110→# (No tracking-specific configuration at this time)
   111→
   112→# ============================================================================
   113→# REMINDER
   114→# ============================================================================
   115→
   116→# Static reminder cadence (responses)
   117→# Reminder will be injected every N responses
   118→TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE=4
   119→
   120→# ============================================================================
   121→# CLEANUP (Session Directory Garbage Collection)
   122→# ============================================================================
   123→
   124→# Enable automatic cleanup
   125→# If disabled, old session directories will never be removed
   126→CLEANUP_ENABLED=true
   127→
   128→# Minimum number of old sessions before cleanup triggers
   129→# Cleanup only runs when old session count >= this value
   130→CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT=5
   131→
   132→# Age threshold for old sessions (days)
   133→# Sessions older than this are candidates for cleanup
   134→CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS=2
   135→
   136→# Dry run mode (log but don't delete)
   137→# Useful for testing cleanup behavior
   138→CLEANUP_DRY_RUN=false
   139→
   140→# ============================================================================
   141→# GLOBAL
   142→# ============================================================================
   143→
   144→# Log level: debug | info | warn | error
   145→# Controls verbosity of log output
   146→#   debug: All messages (very verbose)
   147→#   info: Informational and above
   148→#   warn: Warnings and errors only
   149→#   error: Errors only
   150→LOG_LEVEL=info
   151→
   152→# Override Claude CLI binary path (optional)
   153→# If empty, will search: CLAUDE_BIN env → ~/.claude/local/claude → PATH
   154→CLAUDE_BIN=
