# ============================================================================
# Sidekick Configuration Defaults
# ============================================================================
#
# This file contains all default configuration values for Sidekick.
# Values can be overridden in:
#   - User config: ~/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf
#   - Project config: $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf
#
# Configuration cascade: this file → user → project (later wins)
#
# Format: Shell KEY=VALUE pairs (sourced by bash)

# ============================================================================
# FEATURES - Set to false to disable
# ============================================================================

# Topic extraction: LLM-based conversation analysis
FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=true

# Resume: Generate snarkified resume topic from previous session
FEATURE_RESUME=true

# Statusline: Enhanced statusline with topic and metrics
FEATURE_STATUSLINE=true

# Tracking: Request counting
FEATURE_TRACKING=true

# Reminder: Periodic static reminder injection (depends on tracking)
FEATURE_REMINDER=true

# Cleanup: Garbage collection for old session directories
FEATURE_CLEANUP=true

# ============================================================================
# TOPIC EXTRACTION
# ============================================================================

# Model to use for topic extraction
# Options: haiku, sonnet, opus
TOPIC_MODEL=haiku

# Number of transcript lines to analyze (default: 80 ≈ 3-5 messages)
TOPIC_EXCERPT_LINES=80

# Filter out tool_use and tool_result messages from analysis (reduces noise and tokens)
TOPIC_FILTER_TOOL_MESSAGES=true

# Cadence for high clarity conversations (responses between analyses)
TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH=10

# Cadence for low clarity conversations (responses between analyses)
TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW=1

# Clarity threshold (1-10 scale)
# Conversations with clarity >= threshold use high cadence
# Conversations with clarity < threshold use low cadence
TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD=7

# ============================================================================
# SLEEPER (Background Polling Process)
# ============================================================================

# Enable sleeper process
SLEEPER_ENABLED=true

# Maximum sleeper inactivity timeout (seconds)
# Sleeper will exit after this duration of no transcript activity
SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION=600

# Minimum transcript size change to trigger analysis (bytes)
SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE=500

# Minimum interval between analyses (seconds)
SLEEPER_MIN_INTERVAL=10

# Dynamic sleep interval (based on clarity score)
# Formula: clarity * 2 seconds (capped between min and max)
# - Low clarity (1-2): sleep 2-4 seconds (more frequent checks)
# - Medium clarity (5): sleep 10 seconds
# - High clarity (10): sleep 20 seconds (less frequent checks)
SLEEPER_MIN_SLEEP=2
SLEEPER_MAX_SLEEP=20

# ============================================================================
# RESUME (Session Continuity)
# ============================================================================

# Model to use for resume snarkification
# Options: haiku, sonnet, opus
RESUME_MODEL=haiku

# Minimum clarity score to use previous session's topic
# Only sessions with clarity >= this value are considered for resume
RESUME_MIN_CLARITY=5

# ============================================================================
# STATUSLINE
# ============================================================================

# Token budget threshold for percentage calculation
# Statusline will show percentage of this value
STATUSLINE_TOKEN_THRESHOLD=160000

# ============================================================================
# TRACKING
# ============================================================================

# (No tracking-specific configuration at this time)

# ============================================================================
# REMINDER
# ============================================================================

# Static reminder cadence (responses)
# Reminder will be injected every N responses
TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE=4

# ============================================================================
# CLEANUP (Session Directory Garbage Collection)
# ============================================================================

# Enable automatic cleanup
# If disabled, old session directories will never be removed
CLEANUP_ENABLED=true

# Minimum number of old sessions before cleanup triggers
# Cleanup only runs when old session count >= this value
CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT=5

# Age threshold for old sessions (days)
# Sessions older than this are candidates for cleanup
CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS=2

# Dry run mode (log but don't delete)
# Useful for testing cleanup behavior
CLEANUP_DRY_RUN=false

# ============================================================================
# GLOBAL
# ============================================================================

# Log level: debug | info | warn | error
# Controls verbosity of log output
#   debug: All messages (very verbose)
#   info: Informational and above
#   warn: Warnings and errors only
#   error: Errors only
LOG_LEVEL=info

# Override Claude CLI binary path (optional)
# If empty, will search: CLAUDE_BIN env → ~/.claude/local/claude → PATH
CLAUDE_BIN=

content.content:
1→#!/bin/bash
     2→# common.sh - Shared library for Sidekick hooks system
     3→#
     4→# This file contains all shared functions used by Sidekick handlers and features.
     5→# Functions are organized into namespaces for clarity:
     6→#   - LOGGING: Session-specific logging with levels
     7→#   - CONFIGURATION: Config cascade and feature toggles
     8→#   - PATH RESOLUTION: Scope detection and path management
     9→#   - JSON PROCESSING: JSON parsing and extraction
    10→#   - PROCESS MANAGEMENT: Background process lifecycle
    11→#   - CLAUDE INVOCATION: LLM invocation with isolation
    12→#   - WORKSPACE MANAGEMENT: Temporary workspace creation
    13→#   - UTILITIES: General-purpose helpers
    14→#
    15→# This file is sourced once per hook invocation and should never spawn
    16→# subprocesses (except intentional background processes via process_launch_background).
    17→
    18→# Double-source guard
    19→if [ -n "${_SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED:-}" ]; then
    20→    return 0
    21→fi
    22→readonly _SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED=1
    23→
    24→# Strict mode
    25→set -euo pipefail
    26→
    27→# ============================================================================
    28→# ANSI COLOR CONSTANTS
    29→# ============================================================================
    30→readonly COLOR_RESET='\033[0m'
    31→readonly COLOR_RED='\033[0;31m'
    32→readonly COLOR_GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    33→readonly COLOR_YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
    34→readonly COLOR_BLUE='\033[0;34m'
    35→readonly COLOR_MAGENTA='\033[0;35m'
    36→readonly COLOR_CYAN='\033[0;36m'
    37→readonly COLOR_GRAY='\033[0;90m'
    38→readonly COLOR_DIM='\033[2m'
    39→readonly COLOR_BOLD='\033[1m'
    40→
    41→# ============================================================================
    42→# ERROR TRAP
    43→# ============================================================================
    44→error_trap() {
    45→    local line_number="$1"
    46→    local exit_code="$?"
    47→    echo -e "${COLOR_RED}ERROR: Failed at line ${line_number} with exit code ${exit_code}${COLOR_RESET}" >&2
    48→}
    49→trap 'error_trap $LINENO' ERR
    50→
    51→# ============================================================================
    52→# GLOBAL STATE
    53→# ============================================================================
    54→# Session-specific log file (set by log_init)
    55→_LOG_FILE=""
    56→
    57→# Global log file (always available)
    58→_GLOBAL_LOG_FILE=""
    59→
    60→# Sidekick root directory (set by path_get_sidekick_root)
    61→# Preserve pre-set value (e.g., from tests) while initializing if unset
    62→_SIDEKICK_ROOT="${_SIDEKICK_ROOT:-}"
    63→
    64→# ============================================================================
    65→# LOGGING NAMESPACE
    66→# ============================================================================
    67→
    68→# Initialize logging with dual log support (global + session-specific)
    69→# Sets up:
    70→#   - Global log: ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sidekick.log (always created)
    71→#   - Session log: ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sessions/${session_id}/sidekick.log (only if session dir exists)
    72→# Args:
    73→#   $1 - session_id (UUID format, optional - if empty only global log is used)
    74→# Globals:
    75→#   _GLOBAL_LOG_FILE - set to global log file path
    76→#   _LOG_FILE - set to session log file path (empty if session dir doesn't exist)
    77→log_init() {
    78→    local session_id="${1:-}"
    79→
    80→    # Initialize global log file (always create .sidekick directory)
    81→    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
    82→        local sidekick_dir="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick"
    83→        mkdir -p "$sidekick_dir"
    84→        _GLOBAL_LOG_FILE="${sidekick_dir}/sidekick.log"
    85→
    86→        # Create global log file if it doesn't exist
    87→        if [ ! -f "$_GLOBAL_LOG_FILE" ]; then
    88→            touch "$_GLOBAL_LOG_FILE"
    89→        fi
    90→    else
    91→        _GLOBAL_LOG_FILE=""
    92→    fi
    93→
    94→    # Initialize session-specific log file (only if session dir exists)
    95→    if [ -n "$session_id" ] && [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
    96→        local session_dir="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sessions/${session_id}"
    97→
    98→        # Check if session directory exists (don't create it)
    99→        if [ -d "$session_dir" ]; then
   100→            _LOG_FILE="${session_dir}/sidekick.log"
   101→
   102→            # Create session log file if it doesn't exist
   103→            if [ ! -f "$_LOG_FILE" ]; then
   104→                touch "$_LOG_FILE"
   105→            fi
   106→        else
   107→            _LOG_FILE=""
   108→        fi
   109→    else
   110→        _LOG_FILE=""
   111→    fi
   112→}
   113→
   114→# Log debug message (only shown if LOG_LEVEL=debug)
   115→# Args:
   116→#   $* - message to log
   117→log_debug() {
   118→    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
   119→    if [ "$level" = "debug" ]; then
   120→        _log_format_ansi "DEBUG" "$*" >&2
   121→        _log_to_file "DEBUG" "$*"
   122→    fi
   123→}
   124→
   125→# Log info message
   126→# Args:
   127→#   $* - message to log
   128→log_info() {
   129→    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
   130→    if [[ "$level" =~ ^(debug|info)$ ]]; then
   131→        _log_format_ansi "INFO" "$*" >&2
   132→        _log_to_file "INFO" "$*"
   133→    fi
   134→}
   135→
   136→# Log warning message
   137→# Args:
   138→#   $* - message to log
   139→log_warn() {
   140→    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
   141→    if [[ "$level" =~ ^(debug|info|warn)$ ]]; then
   142→        _log_format_ansi "WARN" "$*" >&2
   143→        _log_to_file "WARN" "$*"
   144→    fi
   145→}
   146→
   147→# Log error message
   148→# Args:
   149→#   $* - message to log
   150→log_error() {
   151→    _log_format_ansi "ERROR" "$*" >&2
   152→    _log_to_file "ERROR" "$*"
   153→}
   154→
   155→# Internal: Write log entry to file(s)
   156→# Writes to both global and session-specific logs (if available)
   157→# Args:
   158→#   $1 - log level
   159→#   $2 - message
   160→_log_to_file() {
   161→    local level="$1"
   162→    shift
   163→    local message="$*"
   164→    local timestamp
   165→    timestamp=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
   166→    local log_line="[${timestamp}] [${level}] [PID:$$] ${message}"
   167→
   168→    # Write to global log (always, if available)
   169→    if [ -n "$_GLOBAL_LOG_FILE" ]; then
   170→        echo "$log_line" >> "$_GLOBAL_LOG_FILE"
   171→    fi
   172→
   173→    # Write to session-specific log (only if session dir exists)
   174→    if [ -n "$_LOG_FILE" ]; then
   175→        echo "$log_line" >> "$_LOG_FILE"
   176→    fi
   177→}
   178→
   179→# Internal: Format log message with ANSI colors
   180→# Args:
   181→#   $1 - log level
   182→#   $2 - message
   183→_log_format_ansi() {
   184→    local level="$1"
   185→    shift
   186→    local message="$*"
   187→    local color=""
   188→
   189→    case "$level" in
   190→        DEBUG) color="$COLOR_GRAY" ;;
   191→        INFO)  color="$COLOR_GREEN" ;;
   192→        WARN)  color="$COLOR_YELLOW" ;;
   193→        ERROR) color="$COLOR_RED" ;;
   194→        *)     color="$COLOR_RESET" ;;
   195→    esac
   196→
   197→    echo -e "${color}[${level}]${COLOR_RESET} ${message}"
   198→}
   199→
   200→# ============================================================================
   201→# CONFIGURATION NAMESPACE
   202→# ============================================================================
   203→
   204→# Load configuration cascade: defaults → user → project
   205→# Sources config files in order, later values override earlier ones
   206→# Globals:
   207→#   All config variables (FEATURE_*, TOPIC_*, SLEEPER_*, etc.)
   208→config_load() {
   209→    local sidekick_root
   210→    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
   211→
   212→    # 1. Load defaults (must exist)
   213→    local defaults="${sidekick_root}/config.defaults"
   214→    if [ ! -f "$defaults" ]; then
   215→        echo "ERROR: config.defaults not found at ${defaults}" >&2
   216→        exit 2
   217→    fi
   218→    # shellcheck disable=SC1090
   219→    source "$defaults"
   220→
   221→    # 2. Load user config (optional)
   222→    # Allow override for testing (e.g., SIDEKICK_USER_ROOT=sidekick-test)
   223→    local user_dir="${SIDEKICK_USER_ROOT:-sidekick}"
   224→    local user_config="${HOME}/.claude/hooks/${user_dir}/sidekick.conf"
   225→    if [ -f "$user_config" ]; then
   226→        # shellcheck disable=SC1090
   227→        source "$user_config"
   228→    fi
   229→
   230→    # 3. Load project config (optional)
   231→    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
   232→        local project_config="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf"
   233→        if [ -f "$project_config" ]; then
   234→            # shellcheck disable=SC1090
   235→            source "$project_config"
   236→        fi
   237→    fi
   238→
   239→    # Validate configuration
   240→    _config_validate
   241→}
   242→
   243→# Get configuration value
   244→# Args:
   245→#   $1 - config key name
   246→# Returns:
   247→#   Config value or empty string
   248→config_get() {
   249→    local key="$1"
   250→    echo "${!key:-}"
   251→}
   252→
   253→# Check if feature is enabled
   254→# Args:
   255→#   $1 - feature name (e.g., "topic_extraction", "resume", "tracking")
   256→# Returns:
   257→#   0 if enabled, 1 if disabled
   258→config_is_feature_enabled() {
   259→    local feature="$1"
   260→    local key="FEATURE_${feature^^}"  # Convert to uppercase
   261→    local value="${!key:-false}"
   262→
   263→    if [ "$value" = "true" ]; then
   264→        return 0
   265→    else
   266→        return 1
   267→    fi
   268→}
   269→
   270→# Internal: Validate configuration values
   271→# Checks for common misconfigurations and invalid values
   272→_config_validate() {
   273→    # Validate LOG_LEVEL
   274→    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
   275→    if [[ ! "$level" =~ ^(debug|info|warn|error)$ ]]; then
   276→        echo "WARNING: Invalid LOG_LEVEL '${level}', defaulting to 'info'" >&2
   277→        LOG_LEVEL="info"
   278→    fi
   279→
   280→    # Validate numeric values are positive integers
   281→    local numeric_vars=(
   282→        "TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH"
   283→        "TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW"
   284→        "TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD"
   285→        "SLEEPER_INTERVAL_ACTIVE"
   286→        "SLEEPER_INTERVAL_IDLE"
   287→        "SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION"
   288→        "SLEEPER_CLARITY_EXIT"
   289→        "SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE"
   290→        "SLEEPER_MIN_INTERVAL"
   291→        "RESUME_MIN_CLARITY"
   292→        "STATUSLINE_TOKEN_THRESHOLD"
   293→        "TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE"
   294→        "CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT"
   295→        "CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS"
   296→    )
   297→
   298→    for var in "${numeric_vars[@]}"; do
   299→        local value="${!var:-0}"
   300→        if ! [[ "$value" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
   301→            echo "WARNING: Invalid ${var} '${value}', must be positive integer" >&2
   302→            return 1
   303→        fi
   304→    done
   305→
   306→    return 0
   307→}
   308→
   309→# ============================================================================
   310→# PATH RESOLUTION NAMESPACE
   311→# ============================================================================
   312→
   313→# Detect execution scope (user or project)
   314→# Determines if running from ~/.claude or project .claude
   315→# Returns:
   316→#   "user" or "project"
   317→path_detect_scope() {
   318→    local script_path
   319→    script_path=$(readlink -f "${BASH_SOURCE[1]}")
   320→
   321→    if [[ "$script_path" == "${HOME}/.claude"* ]]; then
   322→        echo "user"
   323→    else
   324→        echo "project"
   325→    fi
   326→}
   327→
   328→# Get sidekick installation directory
   329→# Returns absolute path to sidekick root (contains sidekick.sh)
   330→# Globals:
   331→#   _SIDEKICK_ROOT - cached value
   332→path_get_sidekick_root() {
   333→    # Return cached value if available
   334→    if [ -n "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT" ]; then
   335→        echo "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT"
   336→        return 0
   337→    fi
   338→
   339→    # Determine root from this script's location
   340→    # This file is at: {root}/lib/common.sh
   341→    local script_dir
   342→    script_dir=$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
   343→    _SIDEKICK_ROOT=$(cd "${script_dir}/.." && pwd)
   344→
   345→    echo "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT"
   346→}
   347→
   348→# Get session-specific directory path (does NOT create directory)
   349→# Args:
   350→#   $1 - session_id (UUID format)
   351→# Returns:
   352→#   Absolute path to session directory: ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sessions/{session-id}/
   353→# Notes:
   354→#   - CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR must be set (Sidekick requires project context)
   355→#   - Session state is always stored at project root in .sidekick/sessions/
   356→#   - This directory is gitignored and contains ephemeral session state
   357→#   - This function does NOT create the directory - use util_create_session_dir() for that
   358→path_get_session_dir() {
   359→    local session_id="$1"
   360→
   361→    # Require CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR - Sidekick only works in project contexts
   362→    if [ -z "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
   363→        log_error "CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR is not set. Sidekick requires a project context."
   364→        return 1
   365→    fi
   366→
   367→    # Return project-local session directory path
   368→    echo "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sessions/${session_id}"
   369→}
   370→
   371→# Get project directory from JSON input or environment
   372→# Args:
   373→#   $1 - JSON input (optional, reads from stdin if empty)
   374→# Returns:
   375→#   Absolute path to project directory
   376→path_get_project_dir() {
   377→    local json="${1:-}"
   378→
   379→    # If JSON provided, try to extract from it
   380→    if [ -n "$json" ]; then
   381→        local from_json
   382→        from_json=$(json_get "$json" ".workspace.project_dir // .workspace.current_dir // empty")
   383→        if [ -n "$from_json" ]; then
   384→            _path_normalize "$from_json"
   385→            return 0
   386→        fi
   387→    fi
   388→
   389→    # Fall back to CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR environment variable
   390→    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
   391→        _path_normalize "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"
   392→        return 0
   393→    fi
   394→
   395→    # Fall back to current directory
   396→    pwd
   397→}
   398→
   399→# Internal: Normalize path (resolve symlinks, remove trailing slash)
   400→# Args:
   401→#   $1 - path to normalize
   402→# Returns:
   403→#   Normalized absolute path
   404→_path_normalize() {
   405→    local path="$1"
   406→
   407→    # Resolve to absolute path
   408→    if [ -e "$path" ]; then
   409→        readlink -f "$path"
   410→    else
   411→        # Path doesn't exist, just clean it up
   412→        echo "$path" | sed 's:/*$::'
   413→    fi
   414→}
   415→
   416→# ============================================================================
   417→# JSON PROCESSING NAMESPACE
   418→# ============================================================================
   419→
   420→# Extract value from JSON using jq
   421→# Args:
   422→#   $1 - JSON string
   423→#   $2 - jq path (e.g., ".session_id", ".workspace.project_dir")
   424→# Returns:
   425→#   Extracted value or empty string
   426→json_get() {
   427→    local json="$1"
   428→    local path="$2"
   429→
   430→    echo "$json" | jq -r "${path} // empty" 2>/dev/null || echo ""
   431→}
   432→
   433→# Extract session_id from JSON
   434→# Args:
   435→#   $1 - JSON string
   436→# Returns:
   437→#   Session ID or empty string
   438→json_get_session_id() {
   439→    json_get "$1" ".session_id"
   440→}
   441→
   442→# Extract transcript_path from JSON
   443→# Args:
   444→#   $1 - JSON string
   445→# Returns:
   446→#   Transcript path or empty string
   447→json_get_transcript_path() {
   448→    json_get "$1" ".transcript_path"
   449→}
   450→
   451→# Validate JSON syntax
   452→# Args:
   453→#   $1 - JSON string
   454→# Returns:
   455→#   0 if valid, 1 if invalid
   456→json_validate() {
   457→    local json="$1"
   458→    echo "$json" | jq empty 2>/dev/null
   459→}
   460→
   461→# Extract JSON from markdown code block
   462→# Handles Claude output that wraps JSON in ```json...```
   463→# Args:
   464→#   $1 - text that may contain markdown-wrapped JSON
   465→# Returns:
   466→#   Extracted JSON or original text if not wrapped
   467→json_extract_from_markdown() {
   468→    local text="$1"
   469→
   470→    # Check if wrapped in markdown code block
   471→    if echo "$text" | grep -q '^```json'; then
   472→        # Extract content between ```json and ```
   473→        echo "$text" | sed -n '/^```json$/,/^```$/p' | sed '1d;$d'
   474→    else
   475→        # Return as-is
   476→        echo "$text"
   477→    fi
   478→}
   479→
   480→# ============================================================================
   481→# PROCESS MANAGEMENT NAMESPACE
   482→# ============================================================================
   483→
   484→# Launch background process with PID tracking
   485→# Args:
   486→#   $1 - session_id
   487→#   $2 - process name (e.g., "sleeper", "cleanup", "analysis")
   488→#   $3 - function name to execute
   489→#   $@ - additional arguments to pass to function
   490→# Creates:
   491→#   ${session_dir}/${name}.pid - PID file
   492→#   ${session_dir}/${name}.log - Log file
   493→process_launch_background() {
   494→    local session_id="$1"
   495→    local name="$2"
   496→    local function="$3"
   497→    shift 3
   498→    local args=("$@")
   499→
   500→    local session_dir
   501→    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
   502→    local pid_file="${session_dir}/${name}.pid"
   503→    local log_file="${session_dir}/${name}.log"
   504→
   505→    # Check if already running
   506→    if process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
   507→        log_debug "Process ${name} already running for session ${session_id}"
   508→        return 0
   509→    fi
   510→
   511→    # Launch background process
   512→    log_debug "Launching ${name} process for session ${session_id}"
   513→
   514→    # Use nohup to detach from terminal and redirect output to log
   515→    nohup bash -c "
   516→        # Source common.sh to have access to all functions
   517→        source '$(path_get_sidekick_root)/lib/common.sh'
   518→
   519→        # Call the function with args
   520→        $function ${args[*]} >> '$log_file' 2>&1
   521→    " &
   522→
   523→    local pid=$!
   524→    echo "$pid" > "$pid_file"
   525→
   526→    log_debug "Launched ${name} with PID ${pid}"
   527→}
   528→
   529→# Check if process is running by PID file
   530→# Args:
   531→#   $1 - PID file path
   532→# Returns:
   533→#   0 if running, 1 if not running
   534→process_is_running() {
   535→    local pid_file="$1"
   536→
   537→    if [ ! -f "$pid_file" ]; then
   538→        return 1
   539→    fi
   540→
   541→    local pid
   542→    pid=$(cat "$pid_file")
   543→
   544→    # Check if PID exists and is running
   545→    if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
   546→        return 0
   547→    else
   548→        return 1
   549→    fi
   550→}
   551→
   552→# Kill process by PID file (graceful then forceful)
   553→# Args:
   554→#   $1 - PID file path
   555→process_kill() {
   556→    local pid_file="$1"
   557→
   558→    if [ ! -f "$pid_file" ]; then
   559→        return 0
   560→    fi
   561→
   562→    local pid
   563→    pid=$(cat "$pid_file")
   564→
   565→    # Try graceful shutdown first
   566→    if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
   567→        log_debug "Sending SIGTERM to PID ${pid}"
   568→        kill -TERM "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
   569→
   570→        # Wait up to 5 seconds for graceful shutdown
   571→        local waited=0
   572→        while kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null && [ $waited -lt 5 ]; do
   573→            sleep 1
   574→            waited=$((waited + 1))
   575→        done
   576→
   577→        # Force kill if still running
   578→        if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
   579→            log_warn "Process ${pid} did not respond to SIGTERM, sending SIGKILL"
   580→            kill -KILL "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
   581→        fi
   582→    fi
   583→
   584→    # Remove PID file
   585→    rm -f "$pid_file"
   586→}
   587→
   588→# Clean up stale PID files (process no longer running)
   589→# Args:
   590→#   $1 - session directory path
   591→process_cleanup_stale_pids() {
   592→    local session_dir="$1"
   593→
   594→    if [ ! -d "$session_dir" ]; then
   595→        return 0
   596→    fi
   597→
   598→    # Find all .pid files
   599→    local pid_file
   600→    while IFS= read -r pid_file; do
   601→        if [ -f "$pid_file" ]; then
   602→            if ! process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
   603→                log_debug "Cleaning up stale PID file: ${pid_file}"
   604→                rm -f "$pid_file"
   605→            fi
   606→        fi
   607→    done < <(find "$session_dir" -name "*.pid" 2>/dev/null)
   608→}
   609→
   610→# ============================================================================
   611→# CLAUDE INVOCATION NAMESPACE
   612→# ============================================================================
   613→
   614→# Find Claude CLI binary
   615→# Returns:
   616→#   Path to claude binary
   617→# Exits:
   618→#   3 if claude binary not found
   619→claude_find_bin() {
   620→    # 1. Check CLAUDE_BIN config
   621→    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_BIN:-}" ] && [ -x "$CLAUDE_BIN" ]; then
   622→        echo "$CLAUDE_BIN"
   623→        return 0
   624→    fi
   625→
   626→    # 2. Check ~/.claude/local/claude (default installation)
   627→    local local_claude="${HOME}/.claude/local/claude"
   628→    if [ -x "$local_claude" ]; then
   629→        echo "$local_claude"
   630→        return 0
   631→    fi
   632→
   633→    # 3. Check PATH using command -v
   634→    local path_claude
   635→    path_claude=$(command -v claude 2>/dev/null || true)
   636→    if [ -n "$path_claude" ] && [ -x "$path_claude" ]; then
   637→        echo "$path_claude"
   638→        return 0
   639→    fi
   640→
   641→    # Not found
   642→    echo "ERROR: Claude CLI binary not found" >&2
   643→    echo "  Checked: CLAUDE_BIN, ~/.claude/local/claude, PATH" >&2
   644→    exit 3
   645→}
   646→
   647→# Invoke Claude with isolation and error handling
   648→# Args:
   649→#   $1 - model name (e.g., "haiku-4.5", "haiku-3.5")
   650→#   $2 - prompt text
   651→#   $3 - timeout in seconds (optional, default: 30)
   652→# Returns:
   653→#   JSON output (extracted from markdown if needed)
   654→# Exits:
   655→#   1 on error
   656→claude_invoke() {
   657→    local model="$1"
   658→    local prompt="$2"
   659→    local timeout="${3:-30}"
   660→
   661→    local claude_bin
   662→    claude_bin=$(claude_find_bin)
   663→
   664→    # Create isolated workspace to prevent hook recursion
   665→    local workspace
   666→    workspace=$(workspace_create "claude-invoke-$$")
   667→
   668→    log_debug "Invoking Claude model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s in workspace ${workspace}"
   669→    log_debug "Using Claude binary: ${claude_bin}"
   670→
   671→    # Change to workspace directory and invoke Claude using project settings
   672→    # This prevents hook recursion by using isolated .claude/settings.json
   673→    local output
   674→    local claude_errors
   675→    local claude_output_file
   676→    claude_errors=$(mktemp)
   677→    claude_output_file=$(mktemp)
   678→
   679→    # Use heredoc to pass prompt, write to temp file to avoid subshell issues
   680→    # Disable exit-on-error temporarily to capture exit code
   681→    set +e
   682→    (cd "$workspace" && timeout "$timeout" "$claude_bin" -p \
   683→        --model "$model" \
   684→        --setting-sources project \
   685→        <<EOF
   686→$prompt
   687→EOF
   688→    ) > "$claude_output_file" 2>"$claude_errors"
   689→    exit_code=$?
   690→    set -e
   691→
   692→    # Read output from file
   693→    output=$(cat "$claude_output_file")
   694→    rm -f "$claude_output_file"
   695→
   696→    # Check if Claude invocation failed
   697→    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
   698→        errors=$(cat "$claude_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
   699→        log_error "Claude invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
   700→        log_error "Command: cd $workspace && $claude_bin -p --model $model --setting-sources project"
   701→        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
   702→        log_error "Stdout: ${output}"
   703→        rm -f "$claude_errors"
   704→        workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
   705→        return 1
   706→    fi
   707→
   708→    # Check for stderr warnings
   709→    if [ -s "$claude_errors" ]; then
   710→        log_warn "Claude stderr: $(cat "$claude_errors")"
   711→    fi
   712→    rm -f "$claude_errors"
   713→
   714→    # Cleanup workspace
   715→    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
   716→
   717→    # Extract JSON from output (handle markdown wrapping)
   718→    local json
   719→    json=$(claude_extract_json "$output")
   720→
   721→    # Validate JSON
   722→    if ! json_validate "$json"; then
   723→        log_error "Claude output is not valid JSON: $json"
   724→        return 1
   725→    fi
   726→
   727→    echo "$json"
   728→}
   729→
   730→# Extract JSON from Claude output (handles markdown wrapping)
   731→# Args:
   732→#   $1 - Claude output text
   733→# Returns:
   734→#   Extracted JSON
   735→claude_extract_json() {
   736→    local output="$1"
   737→
   738→    # Try to extract from markdown code block first
   739→    local extracted
   740→    extracted=$(json_extract_from_markdown "$output")
   741→
   742→    # If extraction changed the output, use it
   743→    if [ "$extracted" != "$output" ]; then
   744→        echo "$extracted"
   745→        return 0
   746→    fi
   747→
   748→    # Otherwise, look for JSON object in output
   749→    # Match from first { to last }
   750→    if echo "$output" | grep -q '{'; then
   751→        echo "$output" | sed -n '/{/,/}/p'
   752→    else
   753→        echo "$output"
   754→    fi
   755→}
   756→
   757→# ============================================================================
   758→# WORKSPACE MANAGEMENT NAMESPACE
   759→# ============================================================================
   760→
   761→# Create isolated workspace (prevents hook recursion)
   762→# Args:
   763→#   $1 - identifier (e.g., "claude-invoke-12345")
   764→# Returns:
   765→#   Path to workspace directory
   766→workspace_create() {
   767→    local identifier="$1"
   768→    local workspace="/tmp/sidekick-${identifier}"
   769→
   770→    # Create workspace directory with .claude subdirectory
   771→    mkdir -p "${workspace}/.claude"
   772→
   773→    # Create isolated settings.json with hooks disabled
   774→    # This prevents hook recursion when Claude is invoked from within a hook
   775→    cat > "${workspace}/.claude/settings.json" <<'EOF'
   776→{
   777→  "hooks": {},
   778→  "statusline": {
   779→    "enabled": false
   780→  }
   781→}
   782→EOF
   783→
   784→    log_debug "Created isolated workspace: ${workspace}"
   785→    echo "$workspace"
   786→}
   787→
   788→# Cleanup workspace
   789→# Args:
   790→#   $1 - workspace path
   791→workspace_cleanup() {
   792→    local workspace="$1"
   793→
   794→    if [ -d "$workspace" ]; then
   795→        log_debug "Cleaning up workspace: ${workspace}"
   796→        rm -rf "$workspace"
   797→    fi
   798→}
   799→
   800→# ============================================================================
   801→# UTILITIES NAMESPACE
   802→# ============================================================================
   803→
   804→# Validate that value is non-negative integer
   805→# Args:
   806→#   $1 - value to validate
   807→# Returns:
   808→#   0 if valid, 1 if invalid
   809→util_validate_count() {
   810→    local value="$1"
   811→
   812→    if [[ "$value" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
   813→        return 0
   814→    else
   815→        return 1
   816→    fi
   817→}
   818→
   819→# Get file size (cross-platform)
   820→# Args:
   821→#   $1 - file path
   822→# Returns:
   823→#   File size in bytes
   824→util_get_file_size() {
   825→    local file="$1"
   826→
   827→    if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then
   828→        echo "0"
   829→        return 0
   830→    fi
   831→
   832→    # Try stat (GNU/Linux)
   833→    if stat --version &>/dev/null; then
   834→        stat -c %s "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0"
   835→    else
   836→        # Try BSD stat (macOS)
   837→        stat -f %z "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0"
   838→    fi
   839→}
   840→
   841→# Create session directory (and parent directories) if missing
   842→# Args:
   843→#   $1 - session_id
   844→# Returns:
   845→#   0 on success, 1 on failure
   846→util_create_session_dir() {
   847→    local session_id="$1"
   848→    local session_dir
   849→    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id") || return 1
   850→
   851→    # Create directory if it doesn't exist
   852→    if [ ! -d "$session_dir" ]; then
   853→        mkdir -p "$session_dir" || {
   854→            log_error "Failed to create session directory: $session_dir"
   855→            return 1
   856→        }
   857→    fi
   858→
   859→    return 0
   860→}
   861→
   862→# Calculate tokens from transcript (rough estimate)
   863→# Uses approximation: 1 token ≈ 4 characters for English text
   864→# Args:
   865→#   $1 - transcript file path
   866→# Returns:
   867→#   Estimated token count
   868→util_calculate_tokens() {
   869→    local transcript="$1"
   870→
   871→    if [ ! -f "$transcript" ]; then
   872→        echo "0"
   873→        return 0
   874→    fi
   875→
   876→    # Count characters and divide by 4
   877→    local chars
   878→    chars=$(wc -c < "$transcript" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
   879→    echo $((chars / 4))
   880→}

   882→# ============================================================================
   883→# PLUGIN LOADER NAMESPACE
   884→# ============================================================================
   885→
   886→# Array to track loaded plugins (feature names)
   887→declare -a _LOADED_PLUGINS=()
   888→
   889→#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   890→# plugin_extract_depends - Extract PLUGIN_DEPENDS declaration from a plugin file
   891→#
   892→# Reads the plugin file and extracts the PLUGIN_DEPENDS variable value without
   893→# sourcing the entire file. Handles both readonly and regular variable declarations.
   894→#
   895→# Arguments:
   896→#   $1 - plugin file path
   897→#
   898→# Returns:
   899→#   Prints space-separated dependency list to stdout (empty if no dependencies)
   900→#   Exit code: 0 always
   901→#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   902→plugin_extract_depends() {
   903→    local plugin_file="$1"
   904→
   905→    # Look for: readonly PLUGIN_DEPENDS="..." or PLUGIN_DEPENDS="..."
   906→    # Extract the quoted value
   907→    local depends
   908→    depends=$(grep -E '^\s*(readonly\s+)?PLUGIN_DEPENDS=' "$plugin_file" 2>/dev/null | \
   909→        sed -E 's/^\s*(readonly\s+)?PLUGIN_DEPENDS="([^"]*)".*/\2/' | \
   910→        head -1)
   911→
   912→    echo "$depends"
   913→}
   914→
   915→#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   916→# plugin_toposort - Topological sort of plugins based on dependencies
   917→#
   918→# Performs Kahn's algorithm to order plugins such that dependencies load first.
   919→# Detects circular dependencies and reports them as errors.
   920→#
   921→# Arguments:
   922→#   Takes associative array names as parameters:
   923→#   $1 - name of associative array: plugin_name -> "dep1 dep2 ..."
   924→#   $2 - name of array to populate with sorted plugin names
   925→#
   926→# Returns:
   927→#   0 on success, 1 if circular dependency detected
   928→#   Populates output array with sorted plugin names
   929→#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   930→plugin_toposort() {
   931→    local -n deps_map="$1"
   932→    local -n sorted_output="$2"
   933→
   934→    # Build in-degree map and adjacency list
   935→    declare -A in_degree
   936→    declare -A adjacency
   937→
   938→    # Initialize in-degree for all nodes
   939→    for plugin in "${!deps_map[@]}"; do
   940→        in_degree["$plugin"]=0
   941→    done
   942→
   943→    # Build graph and calculate in-degrees
   944→    for plugin in "${!deps_map[@]}"; do
   945→        local depends="${deps_map[$plugin]}"
   946→
   947→        if [ -n "$depends" ]; then
   948→            # Split dependencies by space
   949→            for dep in $depends; do
   950→                # Normalize dependency name (hyphens to underscores for comparison)
   951→                local normalized_dep="${dep//-/_}"
   952→
   953→                # Check if dependency exists in available plugins
   954→                local dep_exists=false
   955→                for available_plugin in "${!deps_map[@]}"; do
   956→                    local normalized_available="${available_plugin//-/_}"
   957→                    if [ "$normalized_available" = "$normalized_dep" ]; then
   958→                        dep_exists=true
   959→                        # Add edge: dep -> plugin
   960→                        if [ -z "${adjacency[$available_plugin]:-}" ]; then
   961→                            adjacency["$available_plugin"]="$plugin"
   962→                        else
   963→                            adjacency["$available_plugin"]="${adjacency[$available_plugin]} $plugin"
   964→                        fi
   965→                        # Increment in-degree
   966→                        in_degree["$plugin"]=$((in_degree["$plugin"] + 1))
   967→                        break
   968→                    fi
   969→                done
   970→
   971→                if [ "$dep_exists" = false ]; then
   972→                    log_error "Plugin '$plugin' depends on '$dep' which is not available or enabled"
   973→                    return 1
   974→                fi
   975→            done
   976→        fi
   977→    done
   978→
   979→    # Kahn's algorithm: find all nodes with in-degree 0
   980→    local queue=()
   981→    for plugin in "${!in_degree[@]}"; do
   982→        if [ "${in_degree[$plugin]}" -eq 0 ]; then
   983→            queue+=("$plugin")
   984→        fi
   985→    done
   986→
   987→    # Process queue
   988→    sorted_output=()
   989→    while [ ${#queue[@]} -gt 0 ]; do
   990→        # Dequeue
   991→        local current="${queue[0]}"
   992→        queue=("${queue[@]:1}")
   993→
   994→        sorted_output+=("$current")
   995→
   996→        # For each neighbor, decrease in-degree
   997→        if [ -n "${adjacency[$current]:-}" ]; then
   998→            for neighbor in ${adjacency[$current]}; do
   999→                in_degree["$neighbor"]=$((in_degree["$neighbor"] - 1))
  1000→                if [ "${in_degree[$neighbor]}" -eq 0 ]; then
  1001→                    queue+=("$neighbor")
  1002→                fi
  1003→            done
  1004→        fi
  1005→    done
  1006→
  1007→    # Check if all nodes were processed (no cycles)
  1008→    if [ ${#sorted_output[@]} -ne ${#deps_map[@]} ]; then
  1009→        log_error "Circular dependency detected in plugins"
  1010→
  1011→        # Find which plugins are in the cycle
  1012→        for plugin in "${!in_degree[@]}"; do
  1013→            if [ "${in_degree[$plugin]}" -gt 0 ]; then
  1014→                log_error "  Plugin '$plugin' is part of dependency cycle (in-degree: ${in_degree[$plugin]})"
  1015→            fi
  1016→        done
  1017→
  1018→        return 1
  1019→    fi
  1020→
  1021→    return 0
  1022→}
  1023→
  1024→#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  1025→# plugin_discover_and_load - Discover and source all enabled feature plugins
  1026→#
  1027→# Scans the features/ directory, sources enabled features in dependency order.
  1028→# Plugins declare dependencies via PLUGIN_DEPENDS="dep1 dep2" variable.
  1029→#
  1030→# Globals:
  1031→#   _LOADED_PLUGINS - populated with loaded feature names
  1032→#
  1033→# Returns:
  1034→#   0 on success (even if some plugins fail to load)
  1035→#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  1036→plugin_discover_and_load() {
  1037→    local sidekick_root
  1038→    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
  1039→    local features_dir="$sidekick_root/features"
  1040→
  1041→    # Reset loaded plugins array
  1042→    _LOADED_PLUGINS=()
  1043→
  1044→    # Check if features directory exists
  1045→    if [ ! -d "$features_dir" ]; then
  1046→        log_warn "Features directory not found: $features_dir"
  1047→        return 0
  1048→    fi
  1049→
  1050→    log_debug "Discovering plugins in: $features_dir"
  1051→
  1052→    # Phase 1: Discover all enabled plugins and extract dependencies
  1053→    declare -A plugin_files_map  # feature_name -> file_path
  1054→    declare -A plugin_deps_map   # feature_name -> "dep1 dep2 ..."
  1055→
  1056→    while IFS= read -r -d '' feature_file; do
  1057→        local feature_name
  1058→        feature_name=$(basename "$feature_file" .sh)
  1059→
  1060→        # Normalize feature name for config lookup (replace hyphens with underscores)
  1061→        local config_name="${feature_name//-/_}"
  1062→
  1063→        # Check if feature is enabled in config
  1064→        if config_is_feature_enabled "$config_name"; then
  1065→            log_debug "Discovered plugin: $feature_name"
  1066→
  1067→            # Extract dependencies from file (without sourcing)
  1068→            local depends
  1069→            depends=$(plugin_extract_depends "$feature_file")
  1070→
  1071→            plugin_files_map["$feature_name"]="$feature_file"
  1072→            plugin_deps_map["$feature_name"]="$depends"
  1073→
  1074→            if [ -n "$depends" ]; then
  1075→                log_debug "  Dependencies: $depends"
  1076→            fi
  1077→        else
  1078→            log_debug "Skipping disabled plugin: $feature_name"
  1079→        fi
  1080→    done < <(find "$features_dir" -maxdepth 1 -name "*.sh" -type f -print0 | sort -z)
  1081→
  1082→    # Phase 2: Topological sort based on dependencies
  1083→    local sorted_plugins=()
  1084→    if ! plugin_toposort plugin_deps_map sorted_plugins; then
  1085→        log_error "Failed to resolve plugin dependencies"
  1086→        return 1
  1087→    fi
  1088→
  1089→    log_debug "Load order (after dependency resolution): ${sorted_plugins[*]}"
  1090→
  1091→    # Phase 3: Load plugins in dependency order
  1092→    for feature_name in "${sorted_plugins[@]}"; do
  1093→        local feature_file="${plugin_files_map[$feature_name]}"
  1094→
  1095→        log_debug "Loading plugin: $feature_name from $feature_file"
  1096→
  1097→        # Source the feature file
  1098→        # shellcheck disable=SC1090
  1099→        if source "$feature_file"; then
  1100→            _LOADED_PLUGINS+=("$feature_name")
  1101→            log_debug "Plugin loaded successfully: $feature_name"
  1102→        else
  1103→            log_error "Failed to source plugin: $feature_name (continuing with other plugins)"
  1104→        fi
  1105→    done
  1106→
  1107→    log_info "Loaded ${#_LOADED_PLUGINS[@]} plugin(s): ${_LOADED_PLUGINS[*]}"
  1108→    return 0
  1109→}
  1110→
  1111→#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  1112→# plugin_invoke_hook - Invoke a hook on all loaded plugins
  1113→#
  1114→# Calls {feature}_{hook_name}() for each loaded plugin if the function exists.
  1115→# Collects stdout from all hooks and returns concatenated output.
  1116→#
  1117→# Arguments:
  1118→#   $1 - hook_name (e.g., "on_session_start", "on_user_prompt_submit")
  1119→#   $@ - remaining args passed to each hook function
  1120→#
  1121→# Returns:
  1122→#   Concatenated stdout from all hooks (empty if no output)
  1123→#   Exit code: 0 (hook failures are logged but non-fatal)
  1124→#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  1125→plugin_invoke_hook() {
  1126→    local hook_name="$1"
  1127→    shift
  1128→    local args=("$@")
  1129→
  1130→    local aggregated_output=""
  1131→
  1132→    log_debug "Invoking hook '$hook_name' on ${#_LOADED_PLUGINS[@]} plugin(s)"
  1133→
  1134→    for plugin in "${_LOADED_PLUGINS[@]}"; do
  1135→        # Normalize plugin name for function lookup (replace hyphens with underscores)
  1136→        local normalized_plugin="${plugin//-/_}"
  1137→        local hook_function="${normalized_plugin}_${hook_name}"
  1138→
  1139→        # Check if hook function exists
  1140→        if declare -f "$hook_function" &>/dev/null; then
  1141→            log_debug "Calling hook: $hook_function"
  1142→
  1143→            # Call hook and capture output
  1144→            local output=""
  1145→            if output=$("$hook_function" "${args[@]}" 2>&1); then
  1146→                # Append non-empty output
  1147→                if [ -n "$output" ]; then
  1148→                    if [ -n "$aggregated_output" ]; then
  1149→                        # Multiple outputs - concatenate with newline
  1150→                        aggregated_output="${aggregated_output}"$'\n'"${output}"
  1151→                    else
  1152→                        aggregated_output="$output"
  1153→                    fi
  1154→                fi
  1155→            else
  1156→                log_warn "Hook $hook_function failed with exit code $? (non-fatal, continuing)"
  1157→            fi
  1158→        else
  1159→            log_debug "Hook $hook_function not defined (skipping)"
  1160→        fi
  1161→    done
  1162→
  1163→    # Output aggregated result
  1164→    echo "$aggregated_output"
  1165→    return 0
  1166→}
  1167→
