# Internal: Invoke Codex CLI provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Raw output from Codex CLI
_llm_invoke_codex_cli() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    local codex_bin
    codex_bin=$(llm_find_bin "codex-cli")

    log_debug "Invoking Codex CLI model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Using Codex binary: ${codex_bin}"

    # Codex CLI interface: codex -q -m <model> "<prompt>"
    # -q flag enables quiet/non-interactive mode (only prints final output)
    local output
    local codex_errors
    local codex_output_file
    codex_errors=$(mktemp)
    codex_output_file=$(mktemp)

    # Disable exit-on-error temporarily to capture exit code
    set +e
    timeout "$timeout" "$codex_bin" -q -m "$model" "$prompt" \
        > "$codex_output_file" 2>"$codex_errors"
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    # Read output from file
    output=$(cat "$codex_output_file")
    rm -f "$codex_output_file"

    # Check if Codex invocation failed
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$codex_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Codex CLI invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Command: $codex_bin -q -m $model \"<prompt>\""
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        log_error "Stdout: ${output}"
        rm -f "$codex_errors"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$codex_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Codex CLI stderr: $(cat "$codex_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$codex_errors"

    echo "$output"
}
input.new_string:
# Internal: Invoke Codex CLI provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Raw output from Codex CLI (text content extracted from NDJSON)
_llm_invoke_codex_cli() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    local codex_bin
    codex_bin=$(llm_find_bin "codex-cli")

    log_debug "Invoking Codex CLI model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Using Codex binary: ${codex_bin}"

    # Codex CLI interface: codex -q -m <model> "<prompt>"
    # -q flag enables quiet/non-interactive mode (only prints final output)
    # Output format: NDJSON (newline-delimited JSON)
    local output
    local codex_errors
    local codex_output_file
    codex_errors=$(mktemp)
    codex_output_file=$(mktemp)

    # Disable exit-on-error temporarily to capture exit code
    set +e
    timeout "$timeout" "$codex_bin" -q -m "$model" "$prompt" \
        > "$codex_output_file" 2>"$codex_errors"
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    # Read output from file
    output=$(cat "$codex_output_file")
    rm -f "$codex_output_file"

    # Check if Codex invocation failed
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$codex_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Codex CLI invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Command: $codex_bin -q -m $model \"<prompt>\""
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        log_error "Stdout: ${output}"
        rm -f "$codex_errors"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$codex_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Codex CLI stderr: $(cat "$codex_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$codex_errors"

    # Parse NDJSON output to extract assistant's text response
    # Codex outputs multiple JSON objects:
    # - {"type":"message","role":"user",...} - input echo
    # - {"type":"reasoning",...} - reasoning info
    # - {"type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"output_text","text":"..."}]} - response
    # We want the last message with role="assistant", content[0].text field
    local text_content
    text_content=$(echo "$output" | grep '"type":"message"' | grep '"role":"assistant"' | tail -1 | jq -r '.content[0].text // empty' 2>/dev/null || echo "")

    if [ -z "$text_content" ]; then
        log_error "Failed to extract text content from Codex NDJSON output"
        log_error "Raw output: $output"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$text_content"
}

#   Raw output from Codex CLI (text content extracted from NDJSON)
_llm_invoke_codex_cli() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    local codex_bin
    codex_bin=$(llm_find_bin "codex-cli")

    log_debug "Invoking Codex CLI model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Using Codex binary: ${codex_bin}"

    # Codex CLI interface: codex -q -m <model> "<prompt>"
    # -q flag enables quiet/non-interactive mode (only prints final output)
    # Output format: NDJSON (newline-delimited JSON)
    local output
    local codex_errors
    local codex_output_file
    codex_errors=$(mktemp)
    codex_output_file=$(mktemp)

    # Disable exit-on-error temporarily to capture exit code
    set +e
    timeout "$timeout" "$codex_bin" -q -m "$model" "$prompt" \
        > "$codex_output_file" 2>"$codex_errors"
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    # Read output from file
    output=$(cat "$codex_output_file")
    rm -f "$codex_output_file"

    # Check if Codex invocation failed
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$codex_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Codex CLI invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Command: $codex_bin -q -m $model \"<prompt>\""
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        log_error "Stdout: ${output}"
        rm -f "$codex_errors"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$codex_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Codex CLI stderr: $(cat "$codex_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$codex_errors"

    # Parse NDJSON output to extract assistant's text response
    # Codex outputs multiple JSON objects:
    # - {"type":"message","role":"user",...} - input echo
    # - {"type":"reasoning",...} - reasoning info
    # - {"type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"output_text","text":"..."}]} - response
    # We want the last message with role="assistant", content[0].text field
    local text_content
    text_content=$(echo "$output" | grep '"type":"message"' | grep '"role":"assistant"' | tail -1 | jq -r '.content[0].text // empty' 2>/dev/null || echo "")

    if [ -z "$text_content" ]; then
        log_error "Failed to extract text content from Codex NDJSON output"
        log_error "Raw output: $output"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$text_content"
}

#!/bin/bash
# common.sh - Shared library for Sidekick hooks system
#
# This file contains all shared functions used by Sidekick handlers and features.
# Functions are organized into namespaces for clarity:
#   - LOGGING: Session-specific logging with levels
#   - CONFIGURATION: Config cascade and feature toggles
#   - PATH RESOLUTION: Scope detection and path management
#   - JSON PROCESSING: JSON parsing and extraction
#   - PROCESS MANAGEMENT: Background process lifecycle
#   - LLM INVOCATION: Pluggable LLM providers with isolation
#   - WORKSPACE MANAGEMENT: Temporary workspace creation
#   - UTILITIES: General-purpose helpers
#
# This file is sourced once per hook invocation and should never spawn
# subprocesses (except intentional background processes via process_launch_background).

# Double-source guard
if [ -n "${_SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED:-}" ]; then
    return 0
fi
readonly _SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED=1

# Strict mode
set -euo pipefail

# ============================================================================
# ANSI COLOR CONSTANTS
# ============================================================================
readonly COLOR_RESET='\033[0m'
readonly COLOR_RED='\033[0;31m'
readonly COLOR_GREEN='\033[0;32m'
readonly COLOR_YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
readonly COLOR_BLUE='\033[0;34m'
readonly COLOR_MAGENTA='\033[0;35m'
readonly COLOR_CYAN='\033[0;36m'
readonly COLOR_GRAY='\033[0;90m'
readonly COLOR_DIM='\033[2m'
readonly COLOR_BOLD='\033[1m'

# ============================================================================
# ERROR TRAP
# ============================================================================
error_trap() {
    local line_number="$1"
    local exit_code="$?"
    echo -e "${COLOR_RED}ERROR: Failed at line ${line_number} with exit code ${exit_code}${COLOR_RESET}" >&2
}
trap 'error_trap $LINENO' ERR

# ============================================================================
# GLOBAL STATE
# ============================================================================
# Session-specific log file (set by log_init)
_LOG_FILE=""

# Global log file (always available)
_GLOBAL_LOG_FILE=""

# Sidekick root directory (set by path_get_sidekick_root)
# Preserve pre-set value (e.g., from tests) while initializing if unset
_SIDEKICK_ROOT="${_SIDEKICK_ROOT:-}"

# ============================================================================
# LOGGING NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Initialize logging with dual log support (global + session-specific)
# Sets up:
#   - Global log: ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sidekick.log (always created)
#   - Session log: ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sessions/${session_id}/sidekick.log (only if session dir exists)
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id (UUID format, optional - if empty only global log is used)
# Globals:
#   _GLOBAL_LOG_FILE - set to global log file path
#   _LOG_FILE - set to session log file path (empty if session dir doesn't exist)
log_init() {
    local session_id="${1:-}"

    # Initialize global log file (always create .sidekick directory)
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        local sidekick_dir="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick"
        mkdir -p "$sidekick_dir"
        _GLOBAL_LOG_FILE="${sidekick_dir}/sidekick.log"

        # Create global log file if it doesn't exist
        if [ ! -f "$_GLOBAL_LOG_FILE" ]; then
            touch "$_GLOBAL_LOG_FILE"
        fi
    else
        _GLOBAL_LOG_FILE=""
    fi

    # Initialize session-specific log file (only if session dir exists)
    if [ -n "$session_id" ] && [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        local session_dir="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sessions/${session_id}"

        # Check if session directory exists (don't create it)
        if [ -d "$session_dir" ]; then
            _LOG_FILE="${session_dir}/sidekick.log"

            # Create session log file if it doesn't exist
            if [ ! -f "$_LOG_FILE" ]; then
                touch "$_LOG_FILE"
            fi
        else
            _LOG_FILE=""
        fi
    else
        _LOG_FILE=""
    fi
}

# Log debug message (only shown if LOG_LEVEL=debug)
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_debug() {
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [ "$level" = "debug" ]; then
        _log_format_ansi "DEBUG" "$*" >&2
        _log_to_file "DEBUG" "$*"
    fi
}

# Log info message
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_info() {
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [[ "$level" =~ ^(debug|info)$ ]]; then
        _log_format_ansi "INFO" "$*" >&2
        _log_to_file "INFO" "$*"
    fi
}

# Log warning message
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_warn() {
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [[ "$level" =~ ^(debug|info|warn)$ ]]; then
        _log_format_ansi "WARN" "$*" >&2
        _log_to_file "WARN" "$*"
    fi
}

# Log error message
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_error() {
    _log_format_ansi "ERROR" "$*" >&2
    _log_to_file "ERROR" "$*"
}

# Internal: Write log entry to file(s)
# Writes to both global and session-specific logs (if available)
# Args:
#   $1 - log level
#   $2 - message
_log_to_file() {
    local level="$1"
    shift
    local message="$*"
    local timestamp
    timestamp=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
    local log_line="[${timestamp}] [${level}] [PID:$$] ${message}"

    # Write to global log (always, if available)
    if [ -n "$_GLOBAL_LOG_FILE" ]; then
        echo "$log_line" >> "$_GLOBAL_LOG_FILE"
    fi

    # Write to session-specific log (only if session dir exists)
    if [ -n "$_LOG_FILE" ]; then
        echo "$log_line" >> "$_LOG_FILE"
    fi
}

# Internal: Format log message with ANSI colors
# Args:
#   $1 - log level
#   $2 - message
_log_format_ansi() {
    local level="$1"
    shift
    local message="$*"
    local color=""

    case "$level" in
        DEBUG) color="$COLOR_GRAY" ;;
        INFO)  color="$COLOR_GREEN" ;;
        WARN)  color="$COLOR_YELLOW" ;;
        ERROR) color="$COLOR_RED" ;;
        *)     color="$COLOR_RESET" ;;
    esac

    echo -e "${color}[${level}]${COLOR_RESET} ${message}"
}

# ============================================================================
# CONFIGURATION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Load configuration cascade: defaults → user → project
# Sources config files in order, later values override earlier ones
# Globals:
#   All config variables (FEATURE_*, TOPIC_*, SLEEPER_*, etc.)
config_load() {
    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    # 1. Load defaults (must exist)
    local defaults="${sidekick_root}/config.defaults"
    if [ ! -f "$defaults" ]; then
        echo "ERROR: config.defaults not found at ${defaults}" >&2
        exit 2
    fi
    # shellcheck disable=SC1090
    source "$defaults"

    # 2. Load user config (optional)
    # Allow override for testing (e.g., SIDEKICK_USER_ROOT=sidekick-test)
    local user_dir="${SIDEKICK_USER_ROOT:-sidekick}"
    local user_config="${HOME}/.claude/hooks/${user_dir}/sidekick.conf"
    if [ -f "$user_config" ]; then
        # shellcheck disable=SC1090
        source "$user_config"
    fi

    # 3. Load project config (optional)
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        local project_config="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf"
        if [ -f "$project_config" ]; then
            # shellcheck disable=SC1090
            source "$project_config"
        fi
    fi

    # Validate configuration
    _config_validate
}

# Get configuration value
# Args:
#   $1 - config key name
# Returns:
#   Config value or empty string
config_get() {
    local key="$1"
    echo "${!key:-}"
}

# Check if feature is enabled
# Args:
#   $1 - feature name (e.g., "topic_extraction", "resume", "tracking")
# Returns:
#   0 if enabled, 1 if disabled
config_is_feature_enabled() {
    local feature="$1"
    local key="FEATURE_${feature^^}"  # Convert to uppercase
    local value="${!key:-false}"

    if [ "$value" = "true" ]; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Internal: Validate configuration values
# Checks for common misconfigurations and invalid values
_config_validate() {
    # Validate LOG_LEVEL
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [[ ! "$level" =~ ^(debug|info|warn|error)$ ]]; then
        echo "WARNING: Invalid LOG_LEVEL '${level}', defaulting to 'info'" >&2
        LOG_LEVEL="info"
    fi

    # Validate numeric values are positive integers
    local numeric_vars=(
        "TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH"
        "TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW"
        "TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD"
        "SLEEPER_INTERVAL_ACTIVE"
        "SLEEPER_INTERVAL_IDLE"
        "SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION"
        "SLEEPER_CLARITY_EXIT"
        "SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE"
        "SLEEPER_MIN_INTERVAL"
        "RESUME_MIN_CLARITY"
        "STATUSLINE_TOKEN_THRESHOLD"
        "TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE"
        "CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT"
        "CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS"
    )

    for var in "${numeric_vars[@]}"; do
        local value="${!var:-0}"
        if ! [[ "$value" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
            echo "WARNING: Invalid ${var} '${value}', must be positive integer" >&2
            return 1
        fi
    done

    return 0
}

# ============================================================================
# PATH RESOLUTION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Detect execution scope (user or project)
# Determines if running from ~/.claude or project .claude
# Returns:
#   "user" or "project"
path_detect_scope() {
    local script_path
    script_path=$(readlink -f "${BASH_SOURCE[1]}")

    if [[ "$script_path" == "${HOME}/.claude"* ]]; then
        echo "user"
    else
        echo "project"
    fi
}

# Get sidekick installation directory
# Returns absolute path to sidekick root (contains sidekick.sh)
# Globals:
#   _SIDEKICK_ROOT - cached value
path_get_sidekick_root() {
    # Return cached value if available
    if [ -n "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT" ]; then
        echo "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT"
        return 0
    fi

    # Determine root from this script's location
    # This file is at: {root}/lib/common.sh
    local script_dir
    script_dir=$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
    _SIDEKICK_ROOT=$(cd "${script_dir}/.." && pwd)

    echo "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT"
}

# Get session-specific directory path (does NOT create directory)
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id (UUID format)
# Returns:
#   Absolute path to session directory: ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sessions/{session-id}/
# Notes:
#   - CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR must be set (Sidekick requires project context)
#   - Session state is always stored at project root in .sidekick/sessions/
#   - This directory is gitignored and contains ephemeral session state
#   - This function does NOT create the directory - use util_create_session_dir() for that
path_get_session_dir() {
    local session_id="$1"

    # Require CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR - Sidekick only works in project contexts
    if [ -z "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        log_error "CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR is not set. Sidekick requires a project context."
        return 1
    fi

    # Return project-local session directory path
    echo "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sessions/${session_id}"
}

# Get project directory from JSON input or environment
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON input (optional, reads from stdin if empty)
# Returns:
#   Absolute path to project directory
path_get_project_dir() {
    local json="${1:-}"

    # If JSON provided, try to extract from it
    if [ -n "$json" ]; then
        local from_json
        from_json=$(json_get "$json" ".workspace.project_dir // .workspace.current_dir // empty")
        if [ -n "$from_json" ]; then
            _path_normalize "$from_json"
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    # Fall back to CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR environment variable
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        _path_normalize "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"
        return 0
    fi

    # Fall back to current directory
    pwd
}

# Internal: Normalize path (resolve symlinks, remove trailing slash)
# Args:
#   $1 - path to normalize
# Returns:
#   Normalized absolute path
_path_normalize() {
    local path="$1"

    # Resolve to absolute path
    if [ -e "$path" ]; then
        readlink -f "$path"
    else
        # Path doesn't exist, just clean it up
        echo "$path" | sed 's:/*$::'
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# JSON PROCESSING NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Extract value from JSON using jq
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
#   $2 - jq path (e.g., ".session_id", ".workspace.project_dir")
# Returns:
#   Extracted value or empty string
json_get() {
    local json="$1"
    local path="$2"

    echo "$json" | jq -r "${path} // empty" 2>/dev/null || echo ""
}

# Extract session_id from JSON
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
# Returns:
#   Session ID or empty string
json_get_session_id() {
    json_get "$1" ".session_id"
}

# Extract transcript_path from JSON
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
# Returns:
#   Transcript path or empty string
json_get_transcript_path() {
    json_get "$1" ".transcript_path"
}

# Validate JSON syntax
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
# Returns:
#   0 if valid, 1 if invalid
json_validate() {
    local json="$1"
    echo "$json" | jq empty 2>/dev/null
}

# Extract JSON from markdown code block
# Handles Claude output that wraps JSON in ```json...```
# Args:
#   $1 - text that may contain markdown-wrapped JSON
# Returns:
#   Extracted JSON or original text if not wrapped
json_extract_from_markdown() {
    local text="$1"

    # Check if wrapped in markdown code block
    if echo "$text" | grep -q '^```json'; then
        # Extract content between ```json and ```
        echo "$text" | sed -n '/^```json$/,/^```$/p' | sed '1d;$d'
    else
        # Return as-is
        echo "$text"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# PROCESS MANAGEMENT NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Launch background process with PID tracking
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id
#   $2 - process name (e.g., "sleeper", "cleanup", "analysis")
#   $3 - function name to execute
#   $@ - additional arguments to pass to function
# Creates:
#   ${session_dir}/${name}.pid - PID file
#   ${session_dir}/${name}.log - Log file
process_launch_background() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local name="$2"
    local function="$3"
    shift 3
    local args=("$@")

    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local pid_file="${session_dir}/${name}.pid"
    local log_file="${session_dir}/${name}.log"

    # Check if already running
    if process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
        log_debug "Process ${name} already running for session ${session_id}"
        return 0
    fi

    # Launch background process
    log_debug "Launching ${name} process for session ${session_id}"

    # Use nohup to detach from terminal and redirect output to log
    nohup bash -c "
        # Source common.sh to have access to all functions
        source '$(path_get_sidekick_root)/lib/common.sh'

        # Call the function with args
        $function ${args[*]} >> '$log_file' 2>&1
    " &

    local pid=$!
    echo "$pid" > "$pid_file"

    log_debug "Launched ${name} with PID ${pid}"
}

# Check if process is running by PID file
# Args:
#   $1 - PID file path
# Returns:
#   0 if running, 1 if not running
process_is_running() {
    local pid_file="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$pid_file" ]; then
        return 1
    fi

    local pid
    pid=$(cat "$pid_file")

    # Check if PID exists and is running
    if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Kill process by PID file (graceful then forceful)
# Args:
#   $1 - PID file path
process_kill() {
    local pid_file="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$pid_file" ]; then
        return 0
    fi

    local pid
    pid=$(cat "$pid_file")

    # Try graceful shutdown first
    if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        log_debug "Sending SIGTERM to PID ${pid}"
        kill -TERM "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true

        # Wait up to 5 seconds for graceful shutdown
        local waited=0
        while kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null && [ $waited -lt 5 ]; do
            sleep 1
            waited=$((waited + 1))
        done

        # Force kill if still running
        if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
            log_warn "Process ${pid} did not respond to SIGTERM, sending SIGKILL"
            kill -KILL "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
        fi
    fi

    # Remove PID file
    rm -f "$pid_file"
}

# Clean up stale PID files (process no longer running)
# Args:
#   $1 - session directory path
process_cleanup_stale_pids() {
    local session_dir="$1"

    if [ ! -d "$session_dir" ]; then
        return 0
    fi

    # Find all .pid files
    local pid_file
    while IFS= read -r pid_file; do
        if [ -f "$pid_file" ]; then
            if ! process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
                log_debug "Cleaning up stale PID file: ${pid_file}"
                rm -f "$pid_file"
            fi
        fi
    done < <(find "$session_dir" -name "*.pid" 2>/dev/null)
}

# ============================================================================
# LLM INVOCATION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================
# Pluggable LLM provider system supporting multiple backends:
#   - claude-cli: Claude Code CLI (current default)
#   - codex-cli: Codex CLI
#   - openai-api: OpenAI API via curl
#   - gemini-cli: Google Gemini CLI
#   - custom: User-defined command template
#
# Provider selection via LLM_PROVIDER config variable
# ============================================================================

# Find Claude CLI binary
# DEPRECATED: Use llm_find_bin("claude-cli") instead
# Returns:
#   Path to claude binary
# Exits:
#   3 if claude binary not found
claude_find_bin() {
    # 1. Check CLAUDE_BIN config
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_BIN:-}" ] && [ -x "$CLAUDE_BIN" ]; then
        echo "$CLAUDE_BIN"
        return 0
    fi

    # 2. Check ~/.claude/local/claude (default installation)
    local local_claude="${HOME}/.claude/local/claude"
    if [ -x "$local_claude" ]; then
        echo "$local_claude"
        return 0
    fi

    # 3. Check PATH using command -v
    local path_claude
    path_claude=$(command -v claude 2>/dev/null || true)
    if [ -n "$path_claude" ] && [ -x "$path_claude" ]; then
        echo "$path_claude"
        return 0
    fi

    # Not found
    echo "ERROR: Claude CLI binary not found" >&2
    echo "  Checked: CLAUDE_BIN, ~/.claude/local/claude, PATH" >&2
    exit 3
}

# Find LLM binary for specified provider
# Args:
#   $1 - provider name (claude-cli, codex-cli, gemini-cli, custom)
# Returns:
#   Path to binary
# Exits:
#   3 if binary not found
llm_find_bin() {
    local provider="$1"
    local bin_path=""
    local config_var=""
    local default_name=""

    case "$provider" in
        claude-cli)
            # Use existing claude_find_bin logic
            claude_find_bin
            return $?
            ;;
        codex-cli)
            config_var="LLM_CODEX_BIN"
            default_name="codex"
            ;;
        gemini-cli)
            config_var="LLM_GEMINI_BIN"
            default_name="gemini"
            ;;
        custom)
            config_var="LLM_CUSTOM_BIN"
            default_name="" # No default for custom
            ;;
        openai-api)
            # OpenAI uses curl (no custom binary needed)
            command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1 || {
                echo "ERROR: curl not found (required for openai-api provider)" >&2
                exit 3
            }
            echo "curl"
            return 0
            ;;
        *)
            echo "ERROR: Unknown LLM provider: $provider" >&2
            return 3
            ;;
    esac

    # Check config variable first
    if [ -n "$config_var" ]; then
        bin_path=$(config_get "$config_var")
        if [ -n "$bin_path" ] && [ -x "$bin_path" ]; then
            echo "$bin_path"
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    # Fall back to PATH lookup
    if [ -n "$default_name" ]; then
        local path_bin
        path_bin=$(command -v "$default_name" 2>/dev/null || true)
        if [ -n "$path_bin" ] && [ -x "$path_bin" ]; then
            echo "$path_bin"
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    # Not found
    echo "ERROR: Binary not found for provider $provider" >&2
    if [ -n "$config_var" ]; then
        echo "  Checked: $config_var config, PATH" >&2
    fi
    return 3
}

# Internal: Invoke Claude CLI provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Raw output from Claude CLI
_llm_invoke_claude_cli() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    local claude_bin
    claude_bin=$(llm_find_bin "claude-cli")

    # Create isolated workspace to prevent hook recursion
    local workspace
    workspace=$(workspace_create "claude-invoke-$$")

    log_debug "Invoking Claude CLI model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s in workspace ${workspace}"
    log_debug "Using Claude binary: ${claude_bin}"

    # Invoke Claude using project settings in isolated workspace
    local output
    local claude_errors
    local claude_output_file
    claude_errors=$(mktemp)
    claude_output_file=$(mktemp)

    # Use heredoc to pass prompt, write to temp file to avoid subshell issues
    # Disable exit-on-error temporarily to capture exit code
    set +e
    (cd "$workspace" && timeout "$timeout" "$claude_bin" -p \
        --model "$model" \
        --setting-sources project \
        <<EOF
$prompt
EOF
    ) > "$claude_output_file" 2>"$claude_errors"
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    # Read output from file
    output=$(cat "$claude_output_file")
    rm -f "$claude_output_file"

    # Check if Claude invocation failed
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$claude_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Claude CLI invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Command: cd $workspace && $claude_bin -p --model $model --setting-sources project"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        log_error "Stdout: ${output}"
        rm -f "$claude_errors"
        workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$claude_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Claude CLI stderr: $(cat "$claude_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$claude_errors"

    # Cleanup workspace
    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"

    echo "$output"
}

# Internal: Invoke Codex CLI provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Raw output from Codex CLI
_llm_invoke_codex_cli() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    local codex_bin
    codex_bin=$(llm_find_bin "codex-cli")

    log_debug "Invoking Codex CLI model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Using Codex binary: ${codex_bin}"

    # Codex CLI interface: codex -q -m <model> "<prompt>"
    # -q flag enables quiet/non-interactive mode (only prints final output)
    local output
    local codex_errors
    local codex_output_file
    codex_errors=$(mktemp)
    codex_output_file=$(mktemp)

    # Disable exit-on-error temporarily to capture exit code
    set +e
    timeout "$timeout" "$codex_bin" -q -m "$model" "$prompt" \
        > "$codex_output_file" 2>"$codex_errors"
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    # Read output from file
    output=$(cat "$codex_output_file")
    rm -f "$codex_output_file"

    # Check if Codex invocation failed
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$codex_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Codex CLI invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Command: $codex_bin -q -m $model \"<prompt>\""
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        log_error "Stdout: ${output}"
        rm -f "$codex_errors"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$codex_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Codex CLI stderr: $(cat "$codex_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$codex_errors"

    echo "$output"
}

# Internal: Invoke OpenAI API provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Raw JSON output from OpenAI API
_llm_invoke_openai_api() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    # Get API key from config or environment
    local api_key
    api_key=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY")
    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        api_key="${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}"
    fi

    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        log_error "OpenAI API key not found. Set LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY in config or OPENAI_API_KEY env var"
        return 1
    fi

    # Get endpoint
    local endpoint
    endpoint=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_ENDPOINT")
    endpoint=${endpoint:-https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions}

    log_debug "Invoking OpenAI API model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Using endpoint: ${endpoint}"

    # Build JSON payload (escape quotes in prompt)
    local escaped_prompt
    escaped_prompt=$(echo "$prompt" | jq -Rs .)

    local payload
    payload=$(cat <<EOF
{
  "model": "$model",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": $escaped_prompt
    }
  ],
  "temperature": 0.7
}
EOF
)

    # Invoke API with curl
    local output
    local curl_errors
    curl_errors=$(mktemp)

    set +e
    output=$(curl -s --max-time "$timeout" \
        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        -H "Authorization: Bearer $api_key" \
        -d "$payload" \
        "$endpoint" 2>"$curl_errors")
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    # Check for curl errors
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$curl_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "OpenAI API call failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        rm -f "$curl_errors"
        return 1
    fi
    rm -f "$curl_errors"

    # Check for API errors in response
    if echo "$output" | jq -e '.error' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        local error_msg
        error_msg=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.error.message // .error')
        log_error "OpenAI API returned error: ${error_msg}"
        return 1
    fi

    # Extract content from response
    local content
    content=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.choices[0].message.content // empty')

    if [ -z "$content" ]; then
        log_error "Failed to extract content from OpenAI response"
        log_error "Response: $output"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$content"
}

# Internal: Invoke Gemini CLI provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Raw output from Gemini CLI
_llm_invoke_gemini_cli() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    local gemini_bin
    gemini_bin=$(llm_find_bin "gemini-cli")

    log_debug "Invoking Gemini CLI model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Using Gemini binary: ${gemini_bin}"

    # Invoke Gemini CLI
    # NOTE: This is a placeholder implementation - actual Gemini CLI may differ
    local output
    local gemini_errors
    gemini_errors=$(mktemp)

    set +e
    output=$(timeout "$timeout" "$gemini_bin" \
        --model "$model" \
        <<EOF
$prompt
EOF
    2>"$gemini_errors")
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    # Check for errors
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$gemini_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Gemini CLI invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        rm -f "$gemini_errors"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$gemini_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Gemini CLI stderr: $(cat "$gemini_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$gemini_errors"

    echo "$output"
}

# Internal: Invoke custom command template provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Raw output from custom command
_llm_invoke_custom() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    local custom_bin
    custom_bin=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_BIN")

    if [ -z "$custom_bin" ]; then
        log_error "LLM_CUSTOM_BIN not configured for custom provider"
        return 1
    fi

    local command_template
    command_template=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND")

    if [ -z "$command_template" ]; then
        log_error "LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND not configured for custom provider"
        return 1
    fi

    log_debug "Invoking custom provider with model ${model} and timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Binary: ${custom_bin}"
    log_debug "Template: ${command_template}"

    # Write prompt to temporary file
    local prompt_file
    prompt_file=$(mktemp)
    echo "$prompt" > "$prompt_file"

    # Substitute placeholders in command template
    local command="$command_template"
    command="${command//\{BIN\}/$custom_bin}"
    command="${command//\{MODEL\}/$model}"
    command="${command//\{PROMPT_FILE\}/$prompt_file}"
    command="${command//\{TIMEOUT\}/$timeout}"

    log_debug "Executing: $command"

    # Execute command
    local output
    local command_errors
    command_errors=$(mktemp)

    set +e
    output=$(eval "$command" 2>"$command_errors")
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    # Cleanup prompt file
    rm -f "$prompt_file"

    # Check for errors
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$command_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Custom command invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Command: $command"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        rm -f "$command_errors"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$command_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Custom command stderr: $(cat "$command_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$command_errors"

    echo "$output"
}

# Invoke LLM with pluggable provider support
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds (optional, default: 30)
# Returns:
#   JSON output (extracted from markdown if needed)
# Exits:
#   1 on error
llm_invoke() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="${3:-30}"

    # Get provider from config
    local provider
    provider=$(config_get "LLM_PROVIDER")
    provider=${provider:-claude-cli}

    log_debug "LLM invocation: provider=$provider, model=$model, timeout=${timeout}s"

    # Dispatch to provider-specific function
    local output
    case "$provider" in
        claude-cli)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_claude_cli "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
            ;;
        codex-cli)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_codex_cli "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
            ;;
        openai-api)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_openai_api "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
            ;;
        gemini-cli)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_gemini_cli "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
            ;;
        custom)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_custom "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
            ;;
        *)
            log_error "Unknown LLM provider: $provider"
            log_error "Valid providers: claude-cli, codex-cli, openai-api, gemini-cli, custom"
            return 1
            ;;
    esac

    # Extract JSON from output (handle markdown wrapping)
    local json
    json=$(llm_extract_json "$output")

    # Validate JSON
    if ! json_validate "$json"; then
        log_error "LLM output is not valid JSON: $json"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$json"
}

# DEPRECATED: Use llm_invoke() instead
# Invoke Claude with isolation and error handling
# Args:
#   $1 - model name (e.g., "haiku-4.5", "haiku-3.5")
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds (optional, default: 30)
# Returns:
#   JSON output (extracted from markdown if needed)
# Exits:
#   1 on error
claude_invoke() {
    # Backward compatibility wrapper - delegates to llm_invoke
    llm_invoke "$@"
}

# Extract JSON from LLM output (handles markdown wrapping)
# Args:
#   $1 - LLM output text
# Returns:
#   Extracted JSON
llm_extract_json() {
    local output="$1"

    # Try to extract from markdown code block first
    local extracted
    extracted=$(json_extract_from_markdown "$output")

    # If extraction changed the output, use it
    if [ "$extracted" != "$output" ]; then
        echo "$extracted"
        return 0
    fi

    # Otherwise, look for JSON object in output
    # Match from first { to last }
    if echo "$output" | grep -q '{'; then
        echo "$output" | sed -n '/{/,/}/p'
    else
        echo "$output"
    fi
}

# DEPRECATED: Use llm_extract_json() instead
# Backward compatibility alias
claude_extract_json() {
    llm_extract_json "$@"
}

# ============================================================================
# WORKSPACE MANAGEMENT NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Create isolated workspace (prevents hook recursion)
# Args:
#   $1 - identifier (e.g., "claude-invoke-12345")
# Returns:
#   Path to workspace directory
workspace_create() {
    local identifier="$1"
    local workspace="/tmp/sidekick-${identifier}"

    # Create workspace directory with .claude subdirectory
    mkdir -p "${workspace}/.claude"

    # Create isolated settings.json with hooks disabled
    # This prevents hook recursion when Claude is invoked from within a hook
    cat > "${workspace}/.claude/settings.json" <<'EOF'
{
  "hooks": {},
  "statusline": {
    "enabled": false
  }
}
EOF

    log_debug "Created isolated workspace: ${workspace}"
    echo "$workspace"
}

# Cleanup workspace
# Args:
#   $1 - workspace path
workspace_cleanup() {
    local workspace="$1"

    if [ -d "$workspace" ]; then
        log_debug "Cleaning up workspace: ${workspace}"
        rm -rf "$workspace"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# UTILITIES NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Validate that value is non-negative integer
# Args:
#   $1 - value to validate
# Returns:
#   0 if valid, 1 if invalid
util_validate_count() {
    local value="$1"

    if [[ "$value" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Get file size (cross-platform)
# Args:
#   $1 - file path
# Returns:
#   File size in bytes
util_get_file_size() {
    local file="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then
        echo "0"
        return 0
    fi

    # Try stat (GNU/Linux)
    if stat --version &>/dev/null; then
        stat -c %s "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0"
    else
        # Try BSD stat (macOS)
        stat -f %z "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0"
    fi
}

# Create session directory (and parent directories) if missing
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id
# Returns:
#   0 on success, 1 on failure
util_create_session_dir() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id") || return 1

    # Create directory if it doesn't exist
    if [ ! -d "$session_dir" ]; then
        mkdir -p "$session_dir" || {
            log_error "Failed to create session directory: $session_dir"
            return 1
        }
    fi

    return 0
}

# Calculate tokens from transcript (rough estimate)
# Uses approximation: 1 token ≈ 4 characters for English text
# Args:
#   $1 - transcript file path
# Returns:
#   Estimated token count
util_calculate_tokens() {
    local transcript="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$transcript" ]; then
        echo "0"
        return 0
    fi

    # Count characters and divide by 4
    local chars
    chars=$(wc -c < "$transcript" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
    echo $((chars / 4))
}

# ============================================================================
# PLUGIN LOADER NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Array to track loaded plugins (feature names)
declare -a _LOADED_PLUGINS=()

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# plugin_extract_depends - Extract PLUGIN_DEPENDS declaration from a plugin file
#
# Reads the plugin file and extracts the PLUGIN_DEPENDS variable value without
# sourcing the entire file. Handles both readonly and regular variable declarations.
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - plugin file path
#
# Returns:
#   Prints space-separated dependency list to stdout (empty if no dependencies)
#   Exit code: 0 always
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
plugin_extract_depends() {
    local plugin_file="$1"

    # Look for: readonly PLUGIN_DEPENDS="..." or PLUGIN_DEPENDS="..."
    # Extract the quoted value
    local depends
    depends=$(grep -E '^\s*(readonly\s+)?PLUGIN_DEPENDS=' "$plugin_file" 2>/dev/null | \
        sed -E 's/^\s*(readonly\s+)?PLUGIN_DEPENDS="([^"]*)".*/\2/' | \
        head -1)

    echo "$depends"
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# plugin_toposort - Topological sort of plugins based on dependencies
#
# Performs Kahn's algorithm to order plugins such that dependencies load first.
# Detects circular dependencies and reports them as errors.
#
# Arguments:
#   Takes associative array names as parameters:
#   $1 - name of associative array: plugin_name -> "dep1 dep2 ..."
#   $2 - name of array to populate with sorted plugin names
#
# Returns:
#   0 on success, 1 if circular dependency detected
#   Populates output array with sorted plugin names
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
plugin_toposort() {
    local -n deps_map="$1"
    local -n sorted_output="$2"

    # Build in-degree map and adjacency list
    declare -A in_degree
    declare -A adjacency

    # Initialize in-degree for all nodes
    for plugin in "${!deps_map[@]}"; do
        in_degree["$plugin"]=0
    done

    # Build graph and calculate in-degrees
    for plugin in "${!deps_map[@]}"; do
        local depends="${deps_map[$plugin]}"

        if [ -n "$depends" ]; then
            # Split dependencies by space
            for dep in $depends; do
                # Normalize dependency name (hyphens to underscores for comparison)
                local normalized_dep="${dep//-/_}"

                # Check if dependency exists in available plugins
                local dep_exists=false
                for available_plugin in "${!deps_map[@]}"; do
                    local normalized_available="${available_plugin//-/_}"
                    if [ "$normalized_available" = "$normalized_dep" ]; then
                        dep_exists=true
                        # Add edge: dep -> plugin
                        if [ -z "${adjacency[$available_plugin]:-}" ]; then
                            adjacency["$available_plugin"]="$plugin"
                        else
                            adjacency["$available_plugin"]="${adjacency[$available_plugin]} $plugin"
                        fi
                        # Increment in-degree
                        in_degree["$plugin"]=$((in_degree["$plugin"] + 1))
                        break
                    fi
                done

                if [ "$dep_exists" = false ]; then
                    log_error "Plugin '$plugin' depends on '$dep' which is not available or enabled"
                    return 1
                fi
            done
        fi
    done

    # Kahn's algorithm: find all nodes with in-degree 0
    local queue=()
    for plugin in "${!in_degree[@]}"; do
        if [ "${in_degree[$plugin]}" -eq 0 ]; then
            queue+=("$plugin")
        fi
    done

    # Process queue
    sorted_output=()
    while [ ${#queue[@]} -gt 0 ]; do
        # Dequeue
        local current="${queue[0]}"
        queue=("${queue[@]:1}")

        sorted_output+=("$current")

        # For each neighbor, decrease in-degree
        if [ -n "${adjacency[$current]:-}" ]; then
            for neighbor in ${adjacency[$current]}; do
                in_degree["$neighbor"]=$((in_degree["$neighbor"] - 1))
                if [ "${in_degree[$neighbor]}" -eq 0 ]; then
                    queue+=("$neighbor")
                fi
            done
        fi
    done

    # Check if all nodes were processed (no cycles)
    if [ ${#sorted_output[@]} -ne ${#deps_map[@]} ]; then
        log_error "Circular dependency detected in plugins"

        # Find which plugins are in the cycle
        for plugin in "${!in_degree[@]}"; do
            if [ "${in_degree[$plugin]}" -gt 0 ]; then
                log_error "  Plugin '$plugin' is part of dependency cycle (in-degree: ${in_degree[$plugin]})"
            fi
        done

        return 1
    fi

    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# plugin_discover_and_load - Discover and source all enabled feature plugins
#
# Scans the features/ directory, sources enabled features in dependency order.
# Plugins declare dependencies via PLUGIN_DEPENDS="dep1 dep2" variable.
#
# Globals:
#   _LOADED_PLUGINS - populated with loaded feature names
#
# Returns:
#   0 on success (even if some plugins fail to load)
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
plugin_discover_and_load() {
    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
    local features_dir="$sidekick_root/features"

    # Reset loaded plugins array
    _LOADED_PLUGINS=()

    # Check if features directory exists
    if [ ! -d "$features_dir" ]; then
        log_warn "Features directory not found: $features_dir"
        return 0
    fi

    log_debug "Discovering plugins in: $features_dir"

    # Phase 1: Discover all enabled plugins and extract dependencies
    declare -A plugin_files_map  # feature_name -> file_path
    declare -A plugin_deps_map   # feature_name -> "dep1 dep2 ..."

    while IFS= read -r -d '' feature_file; do
        local feature_name
        feature_name=$(basename "$feature_file" .sh)

        # Normalize feature name for config lookup (replace hyphens with underscores)
        local config_name="${feature_name//-/_}"

        # Check if feature is enabled in config
        if config_is_feature_enabled "$config_name"; then
            log_debug "Discovered plugin: $feature_name"

            # Extract dependencies from file (without sourcing)
            local depends
            depends=$(plugin_extract_depends "$feature_file")

            plugin_files_map["$feature_name"]="$feature_file"
            plugin_deps_map["$feature_name"]="$depends"

            if [ -n "$depends" ]; then
                log_debug "  Dependencies: $depends"
            fi
        else
            log_debug "Skipping disabled plugin: $feature_name"
        fi
    done < <(find "$features_dir" -maxdepth 1 -name "*.sh" -type f -print0 | sort -z)

    # Phase 2: Topological sort based on dependencies
    local sorted_plugins=()
    if ! plugin_toposort plugin_deps_map sorted_plugins; then
        log_error "Failed to resolve plugin dependencies"
        return 1
    fi

    log_debug "Load order (after dependency resolution): ${sorted_plugins[*]}"

    # Phase 3: Load plugins in dependency order
    for feature_name in "${sorted_plugins[@]}"; do
        local feature_file="${plugin_files_map[$feature_name]}"

        log_debug "Loading plugin: $feature_name from $feature_file"

        # Source the feature file
        # shellcheck disable=SC1090
        if source "$feature_file"; then
            _LOADED_PLUGINS+=("$feature_name")
            log_debug "Plugin loaded successfully: $feature_name"
        else
            log_error "Failed to source plugin: $feature_name (continuing with other plugins)"
        fi
    done

    log_info "Loaded ${#_LOADED_PLUGINS[@]} plugin(s): ${_LOADED_PLUGINS[*]}"
    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# plugin_invoke_hook - Invoke a hook on all loaded plugins
#
# Calls {feature}_{hook_name}() for each loaded plugin if the function exists.
# Collects stdout from all hooks and returns concatenated output.
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - hook_name (e.g., "on_session_start", "on_user_prompt_submit")
#   $@ - remaining args passed to each hook function
#
# Returns:
#   Concatenated stdout from all hooks (empty if no output)
#   Exit code: 0 (hook failures are logged but non-fatal)
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
plugin_invoke_hook() {
    local hook_name="$1"
    shift
    local args=("$@")

    local aggregated_output=""

    log_debug "Invoking hook '$hook_name' on ${#_LOADED_PLUGINS[@]} plugin(s)"

    for plugin in "${_LOADED_PLUGINS[@]}"; do
        # Normalize plugin name for function lookup (replace hyphens with underscores)
        local normalized_plugin="${plugin//-/_}"
        local hook_function="${normalized_plugin}_${hook_name}"

        # Check if hook function exists
        if declare -f "$hook_function" &>/dev/null; then
            log_debug "Calling hook: $hook_function"

            # Call hook and capture output
            local output=""
            if output=$("$hook_function" "${args[@]}" 2>&1); then
                # Append non-empty output
                if [ -n "$output" ]; then
                    if [ -n "$aggregated_output" ]; then
                        # Multiple outputs - concatenate with newline
                        aggregated_output="${aggregated_output}"$'\n'"${output}"
                    else
                        aggregated_output="$output"
                    fi
                fi
            else
                log_warn "Hook $hook_function failed with exit code $? (non-fatal, continuing)"
            fi
        else
            log_debug "Hook $hook_function not defined (skipping)"
        fi
    done

    # Output aggregated result
    echo "$aggregated_output"
    return 0
}
